what: Comparison against the analytical terminal half-life
title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: Farrell 2012 tabulate NCA-derived Cmax/AUC. dominant biexponential rate constant (β) can computed analytically Table 3 parameters direct numerical check. Analytical two-compartment half-lives Table 3 point estimates. terminal β half-life computed Table 3 consistent published IgG-class monoclonal antibody half-lives (multi-week).
code: CL <- 0.00784; Vc <- 3.00; Q <- 0.0203; Vp <- 7.50 k10 <- CL / Vc k12 <- Q  / Vc k21 <- Q  / Vp a <- k10 + k12 + k21 b <- k10 * k21 alpha <- (a + sqrt(a^2 - 4 * b)) / 2 beta  <- (a - sqrt(a^2 - 4 * b)) / 2 thalf_alpha_h <- log(2) / alpha thalf_beta_h  <- log(2) / beta  tibble::tibble(   quantity = c("Distribution half-life (α-phase)",                "Terminal half-life (β-phase)"),   value_h    = round(c(thalf_alpha_h, thalf_beta_h), 1),   value_days = round(c(thalf_alpha_h, thalf_beta_h) / 24, 1) ) |>   knitr::kable(caption = "Analytical two-compartment half-lives from Table 3 point estimates.")

title: Farrell_2012_farletuzumab
text: IIV correlation. paper’s Methods section states “Initial model building used diagonal covariance matrix inter-individual variability correlation clearance volume central compartment included starting model.” Table 3, however, reports diagonal ω² values CL, Vc, Vp without covariance entry. model implemented diagonal IIV match Table 3 tabulates; narrative hint starting CL–Vc correlation carried final covariance value published. Per-study residual error. paper reports separate residual-error estimates Phase Phase II studies (Table 3). new binary PHASE2 indicator (0 = Phase , 1 = Phase II) selects model, following pattern used Cirincione_2017_exenatide.R STUDY1 / STUDY5. virtual cohort sets PHASE2 = 1 Phase II study larger cohort (1,750 2,472 samples) drives weekly weekly / Q3W comparison explored Figure 3. Reference weight. paper specifies COV_ST median covariate value study population. Table 1 overall median weight 66.2 kg used; within-final-dataset median (excluding 169 low-dose samples) separately reported. BSA dose scaling. Doses administered mg/m². virtual cohort computes BSA DuBois subject’s weight assuming 163 cm mean height, clipped Table 1 BSA range (1.37–2.24 m²). paper publish per-subject heights. BSA used convert mg/m² doses mg amounts; covariate model (weight effect CL Vc relevant PK scaling). Dose exclusion. final model built 2,303 samples excluding 169 samples 12.5 25 mg/m² due apparent low-dose nonlinearity. packaged model reproduces final-model parameters therefore used simulate exposure two lowest doses; implemented structure strictly linear. Residual-error interpretation. Table 3’s column heading “σ² proportional (% CV)” reports variance SD% last column’s % CV SD. model uses SDs (20.5% / 34.9% / 7.94 µg/mL) nlmixr2 propSd addSd magnitudes.

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Simulate infliximab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Fasanmade et al. (2009) patients ulcerative colitis ACT 1 ACT 2 phase III trials (N = 482). model 2-compartment IV infusion model linear elimination. Covariates final model serum albumin, anti-drug antibody (ATI / ADA) status, sex clearance, body weight sex central volume. Concomitant immunomodulators (azathioprine, 6-mercaptopurine) evaluated statistically significant effect CL.

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
code: set.seed(2009) n_subj <- 500  pop <- data.frame(   ID      = seq_len(n_subj),   WT      = rlnorm(n_subj, log(77), 0.22),   # Median 77 kg, range ~40-177   ALB     = rnorm(n_subj, 4.1, 0.4),         # Mean 4.1, SD 0.4 g/dL   SEXF    = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.392),        # 39.2% female   ADA_POS = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.068)         # 6.8% ADA-positive ) pop$ALB <- pmax(2.0, pmin(pop$ALB, 5.5))

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Standard UC induction: 5 mg/kg IV weeks 0, 2, 6, maintenance every 8 weeks week 46 (8 doses total). Infusion 2 hours.
code: dose_weeks <- c(0, 2, 6, 14, 22, 30, 38, 46) dose_times <- dose_weeks * 7  # convert to days  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 7, by = 0.5),     # Dense first week   seq(7, 364, by = 3.5)    # Twice weekly thereafter )))  d_dose <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = dose_times) %>%   mutate(     AMT  = round(5 * WT, 1),  # 5 mg/kg     EVID = 1,     CMT  = 1,     DUR  = 2 / 24,            # 2-hour infusion in days     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_obs <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 1, DUR = NA_real_, DV = NA_real_)  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Fasanmade_2009_infliximab") sim <- rxSolve(mod, d_sim, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
code: sim_summary <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / 7)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkred") +   geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "darkred", linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Infliximab concentration (ug/mL)",     title = "Simulated infliximab PK (5 mg/kg IV, induction + q8w maintenance)",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 500 virtual UC patients)",     caption = "Model: Fasanmade et al. (2009) Eur J Clin Pharmacol"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Effect of anti-drug antibodies on PK
title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
code: sim_ada <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   mutate(ADA_label = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA-positive", "ADA-negative")) %>%   group_by(time, ADA_label) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_ada, aes(x = time / 7, y = median, color = ADA_label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_color_manual(values = c("ADA-negative" = "steelblue", "ADA-positive" = "tomato")) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Infliximab concentration (ug/mL)",     title = "Infliximab median concentrations by ADA status",     subtitle = "ADA-positive patients have 47% higher clearance",     color = "Immunogenicity",     caption = "Model: Fasanmade et al. (2009)"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Run PKNCA maintenance dosing interval (5th dose, weeks 22–30). Stratify ADA status treatment group NCA summary, required skill’s PKNCA recipe. NCA summary (maintenance interval, weeks 22-30), stratified ADA status
code: nca_conc <- sim %>%   filter(time >= 154, time <= 210, Cc > 0) %>%   mutate(     time_rel = time - 154,     treatment = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA-positive", "ADA-negative")   ) %>%   rename(ID = id) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- pop %>%   mutate(     time_rel  = 0,     AMT       = 5 * WT,     treatment = ifelse(ADA_POS == 1, "ADA-positive", "ADA-negative")   ) %>%   select(ID, time_rel, AMT, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) data_obj <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj,   dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start     = 0,     end       = 56,     cmax      = TRUE,     tmax      = TRUE,     auclast   = TRUE,     half.life = TRUE   ) ) nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #>  ■■■■■■■■                          23% |  ETA:  8s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                  51% |  ETA:  5s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■         81% |  ETA:  2s nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   digits  = 2,   caption = "NCA summary (maintenance interval, weeks 22-30), stratified by ADA status" )

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Fasanmade 2009 publish individual PK per-subject covariate values, virtual population approximates reported distributions rather reproducing : Weight: sampled log-normal around 77 kg median SD 0.22 log scale (≈ 40–177 kg), matching reported range. Albumin: sampled normal around mean 4.1 g/dL, SD 0.4 g/dL, clipped [2.0, 5.5] g/dL. Sex ADA status: sampled independently 39.2% female 6.8% ADA-positive, respectively. pooled ACT 1 / ACT 2 data fully independent, joint distribution reported. correlation WT ALB imposed; paper report one. Dosing regimen uses 5 mg/kg induction + q8w maintenance schedule. Fasanmade 2009 pooled 10 mg/kg arm; 5 mg/kg schedule approved label dose relevant typical use. Race included retained covariate final model.

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Model: 2-compartment IV linear elimination. TMDD. Key covariates: Albumin (strongest, power -1.54 CL), ADA status (+47.1% CL), sex (-23.6% CL -13.7% Vc females). Body weight significant Vc (exponent 0.538), CL (unlike mAb models). Concomitant immunomodulators (AZA, 6-MP) affect PK final model.

title: Fasanmade_2009_infliximab
text: Fasanmade AA, Adedokun OJ, Blank M, Zhou H, Davis HM. Population pharmacokinetic analysis infliximab patients ulcerative colitis. Eur J Clin Pharmacol. 2009;65(12):1211-1228. doi:10.1007/s00228-009-0718-4

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Simulate fremanezumab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Fiedler-Kelly et al. (2019). Fremanezumab fully humanized IgG2 delta-/kappa monoclonal antibody binds CGRP approved preventive treatment migraine. model fitted 13,745 concentrations 2,546 subjects (74 healthy adults 2,474 patients episodic chronic migraine) pooled across seven phase 1 / 2b / 3 studies IV SC administration. structural model 2-compartment disposition first-order SC absorption, absorption lag time, first-order elimination. Weight covariate retained final model, entering allometric power clearance (exponent 1.05) central volume distribution (exponent 1.53) 71 kg reference. intrinsic extrinsic factors examined (age, sex, race, albumin, renal hepatic function, ADA status, injection site, acute / analgesic / preventive medication use, patient vs. healthy subject status) statistically significant absent packaged model. Article: Br J Clin Pharmacol 85(12):2721-2733

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Fiedler-Kelly 2019 (Section 3.1 Supporting Table S1): 2,546 subjects contributed 13,745 concentrations. analysis population primarily Caucasian (79.9%) female (86.1%), median (range) age 43 (18-71) years median (range) body weight 70.8 (43.5-131.8) kg. presence anti-drug antibodies confirmed 0.7% samples significant covariate. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab")$population (note: readModelDb() returns parsed model function, field accessed attribute function body rather live list).

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Simulate 500-subject virtual cohort whose weight distribution matches Section 3.1 Fiedler-Kelly 2019 (median 70.8 kg, range 43.5-131.8 kg). paper publish individual-level weight distribution, use log-normal approximation bounded reported range.
code: set.seed(2019) n_subj <- 500  pop <- tibble(   ID = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, meanlog = log(70.8), sdlog = 0.22), 43.5), 131.8) )

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Replicate two phase 3 therapeutic regimens reported Fiedler-Kelly 2019 Section 3.4: 225 mg SC monthly 12 doses, 675 mg SC quarterly 4 doses. Simulate 12 months daily sampling first cycle weekly samples thereafter.
code: obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 28, by = 1),   seq(28, 364, by = 7) )))  build_events <- function(pop, dose_mg, dose_times, regimen_label) {   dose_rows <- pop %>%     crossing(time = dose_times) %>%     mutate(amt = dose_mg, evid = 1, cmt = "depot", dv = NA_real_)   obs_rows <- pop %>%     crossing(time = obs_times) %>%     mutate(amt = NA_real_, evid = 0, cmt = NA_character_, dv = NA_real_)   bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows) %>%     mutate(treatment = regimen_label) %>%     arrange(ID, time, desc(evid)) }  events_monthly <- build_events(pop,   dose_mg       = 225,   dose_times    = seq(0, by = 28, length.out = 12),   regimen_label = "225 mg SC Q4W")  events_quarterly <- build_events(pop,   dose_mg       = 675,   dose_times    = seq(0, by = 84, length.out = 4),   regimen_label = "675 mg SC Q12W")  events_all <- bind_rows(events_monthly, events_quarterly)

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab")  sim <- events_all %>%   group_by(treatment) %>%   group_modify(~ {     ev <- .x %>% rename(id = ID)     as_tibble(rxSolve(mod, ev, returnType = "data.frame"))   }) %>%   ungroup() #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Replicate Figure 7: concentration-time profiles over 12 months
title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Figure 7 Fiedler-Kelly 2019 shows median 90% prediction intervals fremanezumab concentration-time profiles two phase 3 regimens.
code: fig7 <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(treatment, time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(fig7, aes(x = time / 7)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi, fill = treatment), alpha = 0.2) +   geom_line(aes(y = median, colour = treatment), linewidth = 1) +   facet_wrap(~treatment, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     x       = "Time since first dose (weeks)",     y       = "Fremanezumab concentration (ug/mL)",     title   = "Figure 7 replication - simulated fremanezumab exposure",     caption = "Replicates Figure 7 of Fiedler-Kelly et al. 2019. Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 500)."   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

what: Replicate Figure 8 (qualitative): exposure vs. body-weight quartiles
title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Figure 8 Fiedler-Kelly 2019 shows monotonic decrease steady-state exposure across quartiles body weight regimens.
code: cav_monthly <- sim %>%   filter(treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W",          time >= 11 * 28, time <= 12 * 28) %>%   group_by(id, WT) %>%   summarise(Cav = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%   mutate(interval = "Cav,ss(0-28d)")  cav_quarterly <- sim %>%   filter(treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W",          time >= 3 * 84, time <= 4 * 84) %>%   group_by(id, WT) %>%   summarise(Cav = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%   mutate(interval = "Cav,ss(0-84d)")  cav_bind <- bind_rows(cav_monthly, cav_quarterly) %>%   group_by(interval) %>%   mutate(wt_quartile = cut(WT,                            breaks = quantile(WT, c(0, .25, .5, .75, 1)),                            include.lowest = TRUE,                            labels = paste0("Q", 1:4))) %>%   ungroup()  ggplot(cav_bind, aes(x = wt_quartile, y = Cav)) +   geom_boxplot(aes(fill = wt_quartile), alpha = 0.7) +   facet_wrap(~interval, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     x       = "Body-weight quartile",     y       = "Average steady-state concentration (ug/mL)",     title   = "Figure 8 replication - steady-state exposure by body-weight quartile",     caption = "Replicates qualitative trend shown in Figure 8 of Fiedler-Kelly et al. 2019."   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Paper-reported NCA metrics simulation can compared (Fiedler-Kelly 2019 Section 3.4 Table 3): median terminal half-life approximately 30 days, independent dose regimen; steady state reached approximately 168 days (6 months); accumulation ratio AUC across 225 mg SC Q4W regimen (12th vs. 1st dose, 0-28 days) median = 2.43; 675 mg SC Q12W regimen (4th vs. 1st dose, 0-84 days) median = 1.21. Compute simulated single-dose AUC Cmax first dosing interval last dosing interval, compare ratio published accumulation ratios. PKNCA grouped treatment + id. PKNCA summary treatment / interval (auclast ug*day/mL, Cmax ug/mL). Accumulation ratios: simulated medians vs. Fiedler-Kelly 2019 Table 3. simulated accumulation ratios within approximately 20% published medians; larger deviations motivate re-examination dose schedule sampling grid rather parameter tuning.
code: nca_window <- function(sim, treat_label, dose_amt, start, end) {   sim %>%     filter(treatment == treat_label, time >= start, time <= end) %>%     transmute(       id,       time_rel  = time - start,       Cc,       treatment = paste(treat_label, ifelse(start == 0, "dose 1", "last dose")),       amt       = dose_amt,       WT     ) }  conc_monthly_d1 <- nca_window(sim, "225 mg SC Q4W", 225, 0, 28) conc_monthly_ss <- nca_window(sim, "225 mg SC Q4W", 225, 11 * 28, 12 * 28)  conc_quarterly_d1 <- nca_window(sim, "675 mg SC Q12W", 675, 0, 84) conc_quarterly_ss <- nca_window(sim, "675 mg SC Q12W", 675, 3 * 84, 4 * 84)  nca_conc <- bind_rows(conc_monthly_d1, conc_monthly_ss,                       conc_quarterly_d1, conc_quarterly_ss) %>%   filter(!is.na(Cc), Cc > 0)  nca_dose <- nca_conc %>%   group_by(id, treatment, amt) %>%   summarise(time_rel = 0, .groups = "drop") %>%   select(id, time_rel, amt, treatment) conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time_rel | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = 0,   end     = max(nca_conc$time_rel),   cmax    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- pk.nca(nca_data)  nca_summary <- summary(nca_res) knitr::kable(nca_summary,              digits  = 2,              caption = "PKNCA summary by treatment / interval (auclast in ug*day/mL, Cmax in ug/mL).") nca_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_res$result) %>%   filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("auclast", "cmax")) %>%   group_by(treatment, PPTESTCD) %>%   summarise(median = median(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = median)  acc_table <- tibble(   regimen = c("225 mg SC Q4W", "675 mg SC Q12W"),   `AR_AUC simulated` = c(     nca_tbl$auclast[nca_tbl$treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W last dose"] /       nca_tbl$auclast[nca_tbl$treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W dose 1"],     nca_tbl$auclast[nca_tbl$treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W last dose"] /       nca_tbl$auclast[nca_tbl$treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W dose 1"]   ),   `AR_AUC published (median)`  = c(2.43, 1.21),   `AR_Cmax simulated` = c(     nca_tbl$cmax[nca_tbl$treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W last dose"] /       nca_tbl$cmax[nca_tbl$treatment == "225 mg SC Q4W dose 1"],     nca_tbl$cmax[nca_tbl$treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W last dose"] /       nca_tbl$cmax[nca_tbl$treatment == "675 mg SC Q12W dose 1"]   ),   `AR_Cmax published (median)` = c(2.38, 1.22) )  knitr::kable(acc_table,              digits  = 2,              caption = "Accumulation ratios: simulated medians vs. Fiedler-Kelly 2019 Table 3.")

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Weight distribution. Fiedler-Kelly 2019 publish per-subject weight distribution. sample log-normal around 70.8 kg median SD 0.22 log scale, clipped reported 43.5-131.8 kg range. SC residual error . source paper reports different residual error models IV (proportional, variance 0.0467) SC (combined proportional variance 0.0531 + additive variance 0.204) observations. approved therapeutic route SC vast majority dataset SC, packaged model uses SC combined error model. Users simulating IV administration aware published IV-specific proportional-error reproduced . Vc,SC . source paper estimated separate central volumes IV (Vc,iv = 2.98 L, FIXED) SC (Vc,SC = 1.88 L) administration reconcile biphasic IV profile monophasic SC profile pooled dataset. packaged model uses Vc,SC = 1.88 L primary therapeutic use SC dosing. Simulating IV administration model slightly -represent initial central volume relative source paper. IV dosing exercised vignette. phase 1 IV arm (225 mg 900 mg, 1-hour infusion) supported structurally (omit depot dose, dose directly central) simulated . Race ADA status included simulated covariates retained significant predictors final model.

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Structural model: 2-compartment first-order SC absorption, absorption lag time, first-order elimination, allometric weight scaling CL (exponent 1.05) Vc (exponent 1.53) relative 71 kg reference. Fixed parameters: Q, Vp, F, tlag held fixed source (Section 3.2 rationale: Q Vp estimated IV phase 1 data, F tlag combined IV + SC phase 1 data, fixed pooling phase 2b / 3 trough samples). packaged model preserves wrapping fixed(). IIV: diagonal matrix CL, Vc, ka; source paper reports -diagonal terms estimated. Terminal half-life predicted structural parameters approximately 30 days, matching paper’s reported median.

title: Fiedler-Kelly_2019_fremanezumab
text: Fiedler-Kelly JB, Cohen-Barak O, Morris DN, et al. Population pharmacokinetic modelling simulation fremanezumab healthy subjects patients migraine. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2019;85(12):2721-2733. doi:10.1111/bcp.14096

what: Simulate trontinemab PK in plasma and brain
title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figures 2 3 publication single 10 mg/kg dose cynomolgus monkey.
code: dSimDose <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 10, # dose in mg/kg # nolint: commented_code_linter.     WT = 5, # cynomologus monkey body weight according to the paper     TIME = 0,     EVID = 1,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimObs <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 0,     WT = 5,     TIME = c(5 / 60, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, seq(24, 336, by = 24)),     EVID = 0,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimPrep <- dplyr::bind_rows(dSimDose, dSimObs) Grimm2023Tront <- readModelDb("Grimm_2023_trontinemab") # Set BSV to zero for simulation to get a reproducible result dSimTront <- rxode2::rxSolve(Grimm2023Tront |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = dSimPrep) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfpla_cerebellum', 'etalfpla_hippocampus', 'etalfpla_striatum', 'etalfpla_cortex', 'etalfpla_choroid_plexus' dSimTront$Analyte <- "Trontinemab"

what: Simulate gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain
title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figures 2 3 publication single 20 mg/kg dose cynomolgus monkey.
code: dSimDose <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 20, # dose in mg/kg # nolint: commented_code_linter.     WT = 5, # cynomologus monkey body weight according to the paper     TIME = 0,     EVID = 1,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimObs <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 0,     WT = 5,     TIME = c(5 / 60, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, seq(24, 336, by = 24)),     EVID = 0,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimPrep <- dplyr::bind_rows(dSimDose, dSimObs) Grimm2023Gant <- readModelDb("Grimm_2023_gantenerumab") # Set BSV to zero for simulation to get a reproducible result dSimGant <- rxode2::rxSolve(Grimm2023Gant |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = dSimPrep) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfpla_cerebellum', 'etalfpla_hippocampus', 'etalfpla_striatum', 'etalfpla_cortex', 'etalfpla_choroid_plexus' dSimGant$Analyte <- "Gantenerumab"

title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figure 2 paper.
code: dSim <- bind_rows(dSimTront, dSimGant) dSim$Analyte <- factor(dSim$Analyte, levels = c("Trontinemab", "Gantenerumab"))  ggplot(dSim, aes(x = time, y = sim)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = "Concentration (ng/mL)"   ) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 336, by = 48)) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1e3, NA)) +   facet_grid(~Analyte)

title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figure 3 paper.
code: d_plot_brain <-   dSim |>   select(time, Analyte, starts_with("C", ignore.case = FALSE)) |>   select(-starts_with("Cbrain"), -Cc) |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -c("time", "Analyte"), names_to = "ASPEC", values_to = "AVAL") |>   mutate(     ASPEC =       factor(         gsub(x = ASPEC, pattern = "^C", replacement = ""),         levels = c("hippocampus", "cerebellum", "choroid_plexus", "cortex", "striatum", "csf"),         labels = c("Hippocampus", "Cerebellum", "Choroid Plexus", "Cortex", "Striatum", "Cerebrospinal Fluid")       )   )  ggplot(d_plot_brain, aes(x = time, y = AVAL, colour = Analyte)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = "Concentration (ng/g)"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 72, 168, 240, 336)) +   facet_wrap(~ASPEC)

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Replicate Figure 2 Hu et al. (2026): prediction-corrected visual predictive check (VPC) showing clesrovimab serum concentrations 150 days single 105 mg intramuscular dose, presented overall population panel panel stratified baseline body weight category. original study data (5,850 PK samples 2,942 infants across three trials) publicly available, vignette simulates virtual infant population using covariate distributions approximate study demographics (median age 3.02 months, median weight 5.4 kg, preterm full-term infants). Body weight trajectories follow-generated using weight--age growth standards (combined sex, 0–10 months).

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: weight--age LMS parameters combined sex, 0–10 months (Multicentre Growth Reference Study Group, 2006). LMS formula : weight = M × (1 + L × S × z)^(1/L)
code: who_lms <- data.frame(   age_mo = 0:10,   L = c(0.3487, 0.2297, 0.1970, 0.1738, 0.1553, 0.1395,     0.1257, 0.1125, 0.0998, 0.0875, 0.0756),   M = c(3.3464, 4.4709, 5.5675, 6.3762, 7.0023, 7.5105,     7.9340, 8.2970, 8.6151, 8.9014, 9.1649),   S = c(0.14602, 0.13395, 0.12385, 0.11876, 0.11535, 0.11254,     0.11056, 0.10947, 0.10868, 0.10814, 0.10765) )  # Returns weight (kg) for a given age (months) and individual z-score # using linear interpolation of LMS parameters between whole-month values who_weight <- function(age_mo, z) {   age_mo <- pmax(0, pmin(age_mo, 10))   L <- approx(who_lms$age_mo, who_lms$L, xout = age_mo)$y   M <- approx(who_lms$age_mo, who_lms$M, xout = age_mo)$y   S <- approx(who_lms$age_mo, who_lms$S, xout = age_mo)$y   M * (1 + L * S * z)^(1 / L) }

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Sample N = 500 virtual infants covariate distributions approximating study population.
code: set.seed(1654) # clesrovimab MK-1654 n_subj <- 500  # Gestational age: 85% full-term (37-42 wk), 15% preterm (32-37 wk) is_preterm <- runif(n_subj) < 0.15 GA <- ifelse(is_preterm, runif(n_subj, 32, 37), runif(n_subj, 37, 42))  # Postnatal age at dosing (months); study enrolled infants <= 3 months PNA_0 <- runif(n_subj, 0, 3)  # Individual weight z-score (fixed percentile across follow-up) # Truncated to ±2 SD to avoid extreme weights wt_z <- pmax(-2, pmin(2, rnorm(n_subj, 0, 1)))  # Race: approximate study demographics race_draw <- runif(n_subj) RACE_ASIAN <- as.integer(race_draw < 0.15) RACE_BLACK <- as.integer(race_draw >= 0.15 & race_draw < 0.35) RACE_MULTI <- as.integer(race_draw >= 0.35 & race_draw < 0.40) # White/Other: race_draw >= 0.40 (reference; all indicators = 0)  pop <- data.frame(   ID         = seq_len(n_subj),   GA         = GA,   PNA_0      = PNA_0,   wt_z       = wt_z,   RACE_ASIAN = RACE_ASIAN,   RACE_BLACK = RACE_BLACK,   RACE_MULTI = RACE_MULTI )  # Baseline weight for stratification pop$WT_base <- who_weight(pop$PNA_0, pop$wt_z) pop$wt_group <- cut(   pop$WT_base,   breaks = c(0, 4, 6, Inf),   labels = c("< 4 kg", "4\u20136 kg", "\u2265 6 kg"),   right  = FALSE )

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Observation times every 7 days 0 150 days, plus dose event time 0.
code: obs_times_day <- seq(0, 150, by = 7)  # Dose records (one per subject at time 0) d_dose <- pop |>   mutate(     TIME = 0,     AMT  = 105, # mg, single IM dose     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA   )  # Observation records with time-varying WT and PNA d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n_subj), each = length(obs_times_day)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(obs_times_day, times = n_subj),     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA,     PNA  = PNA_0 + TIME / 30.44, # postnatal age in months     WT   = who_weight(PNA, wt_z) # time-varying weight from WHO curves   )  # Dose records also need WT and PNA at time 0 d_dose <- d_dose |>   mutate(     PNA = PNA_0,     WT  = who_weight(PNA_0, wt_z)   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, PNA, GA, RACE_ASIAN, RACE_BLACK, RACE_MULTI,     wt_group, wt_z)

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Hu_2026_clesrovimab")  # Simulate: rxSolve uses the N=500 subjects' IIV from the model ini() block. # Setting seed for reproducibility. set.seed(2026) sim_out <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: # Attach baseline weight group to simulation output wt_grp_map <- d_sim |>   filter(EVID == 0, TIME == 0) |>   select(ID, wt_group) |>   distinct()  sim_plot <- sim_out |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time > 0) |> # exclude pre-dose time point   left_join(wt_grp_map, by = c("id" = "ID"))  # Overall population quantiles d_overall <- sim_plot |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   mutate(panel = "Overall")  # Weight-stratified quantiles d_strat <- sim_plot |>   group_by(time, wt_group) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   rename(panel = wt_group)

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: ggplot(d_overall, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10(     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 30)) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (days)",     y = "Clesrovimab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "Overall population\nMedian and 90% prediction interval"   ) +   theme_bw()

code: ggplot(d_strat, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~panel, nrow = 1) +   scale_y_log10(     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 60)) +   labs(     x = "Time after dose (days)",     y = "Clesrovimab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "By baseline body weight category\nMedian and 90% prediction interval"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Virtual population: 500 infants GA, postnatal age, weight percentile, race sampled approximate three-study population (MK-1654-002, CLEVER, SMART). Time-varying weight: Individual weight trajectories generated using weight--age growth standards (LMS method), subject assigned fixed z-score (growth percentile) enrollment. IIV: Simulated using published omega^2 values Ka (23.5% CV), CL/F (14.4% CV), Vc/F (8.12% CV; note high shrinkage 71.9% original analysis). Q/F Vp/F IIV final model. Residual error: Combined proportional (14.3%) additive (0.231 µg/mL). prediction intervals reflect inter-individual variability residual error final population PK model.

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Hu Z, Hellmann F, Zang X, et al. Population Pharmacokinetics Clesrovimab Preterm Full-Term Infants. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2026;119(4):1036-1046. doi:10.1002/cpt.70199

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Simulate ixekizumab serum concentration–time profiles using final paediatric population PK model Jackson et al. (2022) patients aged 6 <18 years moderate--severe plaque psoriasis enrolled IXORA-PEDS (NCT03073200). model two-compartment linear PK model first-order subcutaneous absorption, allometric body-weight effect CL, Q, V2, V3, log-linear ADA-titre effect CL. Citation: Jackson K, Chua L, Velez de Mendizabal N, et al. Population pharmacokinetic exposure-efficacy analysis ixekizumab paediatric patients moderate--severe plaque psoriasis (IXORA-PEDS). Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2022;88(3):1074-1086. doi:10.1111/bcp.15034 Article: https://doi.org/10.1111/bcp.15034

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: IXORA-PEDS Phase 3, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial conducted paediatric patients aged 6 <18 years moderate--severe plaque psoriasis (PASI ≥ 12, sPGA ≥ 3, BSA ≥ 10% screening baseline). population PK analysis dataset comprised 558 measurable ixekizumab serum concentrations 184 patients: 4 patients <25 kg (2.2%), 45 patients 25–50 kg (24.5%), 135 patients >50 kg (73.4%). Overall baseline age ranged 6–17 years (median 15 years >50 kg group, 10 years 25–50 kg group, 7 years <25 kg group), baseline weight 21.5–136 kg, 56.5% female. majority white (81.5%), 3.26% Black/African American, 3.26% Asian, 1.63% American Indian Alaska Native, 7.61% , 2.72% missing race. Regions: US 37.5%, Europe 41.3%, Rest World 21.2%. Baseline demographics reproduced Jackson 2022 Table 1. approved weight-based Q4W regimens 20 mg (<25 kg), 40 mg (25–50 kg), 80 mg (>50 kg), initial 40/80/160 mg loading dose respectively. Programmatic access: readModelDb("Jackson_2022_ixekizumab")$population.

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: IXORA-PEDS patient-level data publicly available, figures use virtual paediatric cohort whose weight, weight-group, ADA distributions approximate Jackson 2022 Table 1 ADA incidence reported Results section 3.1. Simulated cohort summary (compare Jackson 2022 Table 1 weight-group means Results 3.1 ADA incidence).
code: set.seed(20220301) # IXORA-PEDS paper month n_subj <- 184 # match dataset size  # Weight-group proportions from Jackson 2022 Table 1 (overall column) # <25 kg: 4/184 = 2.2%; 25-50 kg: 45/184 = 24.5%; >50 kg: 135/184 = 73.4% u <- runif(n_subj) weight_group <- cut(   u,   breaks = c(-Inf, 4 / 184, 49 / 184, Inf),   labels = c("<25 kg", "25-50 kg", ">50 kg"),   right  = TRUE )  # Sample body weights inside each group to match the reported mean +/- SD # from Jackson 2022 Table 1. Clip to the reported per-group ranges. simulate_weight <- function(group) {   switch(as.character(group),     "<25 kg"   = pmin(22.6, pmax(21.5, rnorm(1, 21.9, 0.520))),     "25-50 kg" = pmin(50.0, pmax(25.0, rnorm(1, 37.7, 8.10))),     ">50 kg"   = pmin(136.0, pmax(50.1, rnorm(1, 71.3, 19.5)))   ) } WT <- vapply(weight_group, simulate_weight, numeric(1))  # ADA titre: 85.8% ADA-negative (ADA_TITER = 1); 14.2% TE-ADA-positive with # titres in three bands per Results section 3.1: #   - Low    (<1:160)        : 52.6% of ADA+ samples #   - Moderate (>=1:160, <1:1280): 38.5% #   - High     (>=1:1280)    :  9.0% ada_pos <- runif(n_subj) < 0.142 ada_titre <- rep(1, n_subj) n_pos <- sum(ada_pos) if (n_pos > 0) {   band <- sample(c("low", "mod", "high"),     size    = n_pos,     replace = TRUE,     prob    = c(0.526, 0.385, 0.090)   )   titre_sample <- function(b) {     switch(b,       low  = sample(c(10, 20, 40, 80), 1),          # 1:10 to <1:160       mod  = sample(c(160, 320, 640), 1),           # 1:160 to <1:1280       high = sample(c(1280, 2560), 1)               # >=1:1280     )   }   ada_titre[ada_pos] <- vapply(band, titre_sample, numeric(1)) }  cohort <- tibble(   ID           = seq_len(n_subj),   weight_group = weight_group,   WT           = WT,   ADA_TITER    = ada_titre )  summary_tbl <- cohort |>   group_by(weight_group) |>   summarise(     n           = dplyr::n(),     mean_WT     = mean(WT),     median_WT   = median(WT),     pct_ada_pos = 100 * mean(ADA_TITER > 1),     .groups     = "drop"   ) knitr::kable(summary_tbl, digits = 2,              caption = "Simulated cohort summary (compare with Jackson 2022 Table 1 weight-group means and Results 3.1 ADA incidence).")

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Weight-based Q4W regimen per IXORA-PEDS: loading dose Week 0 (40/80/160 mg <25/25–50/>50 kg), maintenance Q4W (20/40/80 mg). Simulate Week 24 (6 maintenance doses) trough Week 12 (co-primary endpoint timepoint) reflects steady state. Times hours (model unit).
code: # Weeks -> hours conversion hours_per_week <- 7 * 24  maint_doses <- function(group) {   switch(as.character(group), "<25 kg" = 20, "25-50 kg" = 40, ">50 kg" = 80) } load_doses <- function(group) {   switch(as.character(group), "<25 kg" = 40, "25-50 kg" = 80, ">50 kg" = 160) }  dose_weeks    <- c(0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20) dose_times_hr <- dose_weeks * hours_per_week obs_weeks     <- sort(unique(c(seq(0, 24, by = 0.5), 1, 9))) # dense early + the Week 1 and Week 9 PK addendum timepoints obs_times_hr  <- obs_weeks * hours_per_week  d_dose <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(dose_idx = seq_along(dose_times_hr)) |>   mutate(     TIME = dose_times_hr[dose_idx],     AMT  = ifelse(dose_idx == 1,                   vapply(weight_group, load_doses, numeric(1)),                   vapply(weight_group, maint_doses, numeric(1))),     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA_real_   ) |>   select(-dose_idx)  d_obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times_hr) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   as.data.frame()

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Jackson_2022_ixekizumab") set.seed(42) sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Figure 1 — Mean ixekizumab concentration vs time by weight group (Weeks 0–12)
title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Jackson 2022 Figure 1 plots mean (±SD) trough-observed concentrations 25–50 kg >50 kg groups Week 12. simulated curves show mean (5th/95th percentile ribbon) simulated ixekizumab concentrations window, stratified baseline weight group.
code: sim_plot <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   left_join(cohort |> select(ID, weight_group), by = c("id" = "ID")) |>   filter(time > 0, time <= 12 * hours_per_week, !is.na(Cc)) |>   mutate(week = time / hours_per_week)  fig1 <- sim_plot |>   group_by(weight_group, week) |>   summarise(     mean_Cc = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     Q05     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(fig1, aes(x = week, y = mean_Cc, colour = weight_group, fill = weight_group)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_continuous() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, by = 4)) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Ixekizumab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "Figure 1 replication: mean concentration by weight group",     subtitle = "Simulated Weeks 0\u201312 (loading + Q4W maintenance); mean and 5\u201395th percentile",     caption = "Replicates Figure 1 of Jackson 2022."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure 3A — Week 12 trough concentration vs body weight
title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
code: wk12_time <- 12 * hours_per_week sim_wk12 <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(abs(time - wk12_time) < 1e-6) |>   left_join(cohort |> select(ID, weight_group, WT), by = c("id" = "ID"))  ggplot(sim_wk12, aes(x = WT, y = Cc, colour = weight_group)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Baseline body weight (kg)",     y = "Week 12 trough concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "Figure 3A replication: Week 12 model-predicted trough vs body weight",     caption = "Replicates Figure 3A of Jackson 2022."   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Steady-state trough by weight group (Weeks 8 and 12)
title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Jackson 2022 Results section 3.1 reports mean trough concentrations predicted steady state (Weeks 8 12) 25–50 kg >50 kg groups range 3.20–3.33 µg/mL. Insufficient PK data available summarise <25 kg group. Simulated mean trough concentrations Weeks 8 12. Compare Jackson 2022 Results 3.1: reported means 3.20–3.33 μg/mL 25–50 kg >50 kg groups; insufficient data <25 kg group.
code: trough_weeks <- c(8, 12) trough_times <- trough_weeks * hours_per_week  trough_tbl <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time %in% trough_times) |>   left_join(cohort |> select(ID, weight_group), by = c("id" = "ID")) |>   group_by(weight_group, week = time / hours_per_week) |>   summarise(     mean_trough  = mean(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     sd_trough    = sd(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     median_trough = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups      = "drop"   )  knitr::kable(trough_tbl, digits = 2,              caption = paste(                "Simulated mean trough concentrations at Weeks 8 and 12.",                "Compare with Jackson 2022 Results 3.1: reported means 3.20\u20133.33 \u03bcg/mL",                "in the 25\u201350 kg and >50 kg groups; insufficient data in the <25 kg group."              ))

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Run PKNCA steady-state dosing interval Week 8 Week 12 doses (.e., Week 8 dose Week 12 dose, 4-week window). interval Jackson 2022 reports steady-state trough concentrations. Simulated steady-state (Week 8–Week 12) NCA weight group. Cmin trough Week 12; compare Jackson 2022 Results 3.1: reported mean trough 3.20–3.33 μg/mL 25–50 >50 kg groups.
code: ss_start <- 8  * hours_per_week ss_end   <- 12 * hours_per_week  nca_conc <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, Cc > 0) |>   left_join(cohort |> select(ID, weight_group), by = c("id" = "ID")) |>   mutate(time_rel = time - ss_start,          treatment = paste0("IXE_Q4W_", weight_group)) |>   rename(ID = id) |>   select(ID, time_rel, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- cohort |>   mutate(time_rel = 0,          AMT      = vapply(weight_group, maint_doses, numeric(1)),          treatment = paste0("IXE_Q4W_", weight_group)) |>   select(ID, time_rel, AMT, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + ID) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(nca_dose, AMT ~ time_rel | treatment + ID)  data_obj <- PKNCAdata(   conc_obj,   dose_obj,   intervals = data.frame(     start   = 0,     end     = 4 * hours_per_week,     cmax    = TRUE,     tmax    = TRUE,     cmin    = TRUE,     auclast = TRUE,     cav     = TRUE   ) )  nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   digits  = 2,   caption = paste(     "Simulated steady-state (Week 8\u2013Week 12) NCA by weight group.",     "Cmin is the trough at Week 12; compare with Jackson 2022 Results 3.1:",     "reported mean trough 3.20\u20133.33 \u03bcg/mL in the 25\u201350 and >50 kg groups."   ) )

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Week 12 mean trough: simulated vs Jackson 2022 Results 3.1. Simulated Week 12 mean troughs track published 3.20–3.33 µg/mL range within reasonable margin; residual difference driven differences virtual cohort’s weight distribution actual IXORA-PEDS cohort.
code: pub <- tibble(   weight_group = c("<25 kg", "25-50 kg", ">50 kg"),   published_mean_trough = c(     NA_real_,  # insufficient data in <25 kg group     mean(c(3.20, 3.33)),     mean(c(3.20, 3.33))   ),   published_range = c(     "Insufficient data",     "3.20-3.33",     "3.20-3.33"   ) ) sim_trough <- trough_tbl |>   filter(week == 12) |>   select(weight_group, simulated_mean_trough = mean_trough)  comparison <- pub |>   left_join(sim_trough, by = "weight_group") |>   mutate(pct_diff = 100 * (simulated_mean_trough - published_mean_trough) / published_mean_trough)  knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 2,              caption = "Week 12 mean trough: simulated vs Jackson 2022 Results 3.1.")

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Patient-level data unavailable. Weight distributions within group drawn Jackson 2022 Table 1 mean ± SD clipped reported range; actual IXORA-PEDS per-subject weights published. ADA-titre sampling. 14.2% patients flagged TE-ADA positive per Results section 3.1; per-band split (52.6% low / 38.5% moderate / 9.0% high titre) applies paper’s band-level percentages uniformly patient level, whereas paper reports sample level. ADA-negative patients (85.8%) carry ADA_TITER = 1 log-linear effect zero. Time-varying ADA modelled. paper uses time-matched ADA titre publish per-subject longitudinal trajectories; virtual subject carries single titre full follow-. Baseline weight . Jackson 2022 used baseline weight (mean percent change 1.6% first 12 weeks; 23% ≥±10% change 108 weeks). vignette uses single baseline weight per subject, matching paper. Residual error central compartment. paper reports one proportional residual error (27.7%); additive residual error reported added . Site injection modelled. paper evaluated injection site covariate retain final paediatric model. Dosing regimen. IXORA-PEDS used approved Q4W regimens (loading dose followed Q4W maintenance). Ongoing open-label periods beyond Week 12 simulated beyond Week 24.

title: Jackson_2022_ixekizumab
text: Jackson K, Chua L, Velez de Mendizabal N, et al. Population pharmacokinetic exposure-efficacy analysis ixekizumab paediatric patients moderate--severe plaque psoriasis (IXORA-PEDS). Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2022;88(3):1074-1086. doi:10.1111/bcp.15034

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Astegolimab anti-ST2 IgG2 monoclonal antibody developed severe asthma. Kotani et al. (2022) fit two-compartment population PK model first-order subcutaneous (SC) absorption first-order elimination Zenyatta (NCT02918019) Phase 2b data 368 adult patients. Structurally, model estimates apparent clearance (CL/F), central volume (Vc/F), peripheral volume (Vp/F), intercompartmental clearance (Q/F), absorption rate (ka). Shared allometric exponents body weight fit {CL, Q} {Vc, Vp}. Baseline CRCL (MDRD eGFR) baseline blood eosinophil count enter CL power covariates, binary indicator 70 mg dose arm scales relative bioavailability (Frel) −15.3% Box-Cox transformed IIV per Petersson et al. (2009). Citation: Kotani N, Dolton M, Svensson RJ, et al. Population Pharmacokinetics Exposure-Response Relationships Astegolimab Patients Severe Asthma. J Clin Pharmacol. 2022;62(7):905-917. doi:10.1002/jcph.2021 Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/jcph.2021

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: PK analysis data set comprises 368 adults severe asthma medium- high-dose inhaled corticosteroids plus least one additional controller least one exacerbation prior 12 months. Median baseline characteristics (Table 1): age 53 years (18–75), body weight 79 kg (43–130), CRCL 87.9 mL/min/1.73 m² (MDRD eGFR), blood eosinophil count 180 cells/µL. 66.8% female; 84.0% White, 5.4% Black, 4.6% Asian, 4.6% Native American, 1.4% multiple races. Treatment-emergent anti-drug antibodies observed 7.3% patients. Patients randomized 70, 210, 490 mg SC every 4 weeks (Q4W) 52 weeks. population metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Kotani_2022_astegolimab")$population.

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: parameter values functional forms come Kotani et al. (2022) J Clin Pharmacol 62(7):905-917. -file comments next ini() entry record row-level source; table collects .

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Individual-level Zenyatta data public. Simulate 450 virtual patients (150 per dose arm) whose covariate distributions approximate Table 1.
code: set.seed(2022) n_per_dose <- 150 doses <- c(70, 210, 490) n_subj <- n_per_dose * length(doses)  pop <- tibble(   ID        = seq_len(n_subj),   dose_mg   = rep(doses, each = n_per_dose),   # Body weight ~ lognormal matching median 79 kg and range 43-130 kg   WT        = pmin(130, pmax(43, rlnorm(n_subj, log(79), 0.22))),   # CRCL (MDRD eGFR) ~ normal matching median 87.9; clip to observed physiological range   CRCL      = pmin(160, pmax(30, rnorm(n_subj, 87.9, 20))),   # Blood eosinophils: right-skewed, median 180 cells/uL   EOS      = pmin(2000, pmax(5, rlnorm(n_subj, log(180), 0.9))),   DOSE_70MG = as.integer(dose_mg == 70) ) pop$treatment <- factor(   paste0(pop$dose_mg, " mg Q4W"),   levels = c("70 mg Q4W", "210 mg Q4W", "490 mg Q4W") )

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Q4W dosing 52 weeks (14 doses). Weekly sampling week 60.
code: dose_times <- seq(0, by = 28, length.out = 14)      # days obs_times  <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 28, by = 3.5),   seq(28, 420, by = 7) )))  d_dose <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times) |>   mutate(AMT = dose_mg, EVID = 1, CMT = "depot", DV = NA_real_)  d_obs <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times) |>   mutate(AMT = 0, EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_)  events <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV,          WT, CRCL, EOS, DOSE_70MG, treatment, dose_mg)

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Kotani_2022_astegolimab") sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

what: Replicate published figure — concentration–time by dose group
title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Kotani et al. (2022) Figure 2 shows observed vs. model-predicted astegolimab concentrations time 70, 210, 490 mg SC Q4W. plot analogous VPC packaged model.
code: treatment_map <- pop |> select(ID, treatment)  sim_plot <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time > 0) |>   left_join(treatment_map, by = c("id" = "ID"))  vpc <- sim_plot |>   group_by(time, treatment) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time / 7, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~treatment) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Astegolimab serum concentration (\u00b5g/mL)",     title = "Astegolimab VPC by dose group (SC Q4W x 14 doses)",     caption = "Replicates the dose-stratified VPC panels in Kotani 2022 Figure 2."   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Compute NCA first dosing interval (days 0–28) using PKNCA dose group treatment grouping variable. Simulated NCA (0–28 day dosing interval) Zenyatta dose arm.
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= 0, time <= 28) |>   left_join(treatment_map, by = c("id" = "ID")) |>   transmute(id = id, time = time, Cc = Cc, treatment = treatment)  dose_df <- events |>   filter(EVID == 1, TIME == 0) |>   transmute(id = ID, time = TIME, amt = AMT, treatment = treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 0,   end      = 28,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- suppressWarnings(PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■             66% |  ETA:  1s nca_summary <- summary(nca_res) knitr::kable(   nca_summary,   caption = "Simulated NCA (0–28 day dosing interval) by Zenyatta dose arm." )

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Kotani et al. report terminal half-life 19.6 days (Abstract / Results). model’s analytical λz computed disposition micro-constants (kel, k12, k21) smaller root characteristic equation.
code: CL <- 0.244; Vc <- 0.614; Q <- 0.171; Vp <- 2.74 kel <- CL / Vc; k12 <- Q / Vc; k21 <- Q / Vp s   <- kel + k12 + k21 lambda_z <- 0.5 * (s - sqrt(s^2 - 4 * kel * k21)) cat(sprintf("Analytical terminal t1/2 = %.2f days (published 19.6).\n",             log(2) / lambda_z)) #> Analytical terminal t1/2 = 19.65 days (published 19.6).

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Kotani et al. tabulate NCA metrics per dose group, report terminal half-life (Abstract) state Discussion exposure dose-proportional 70–490 mg range adjusting 70 mg Frel offset. simulated median Cmax AUClast first dose scale dose × Frel (Frel = 0.847 70 mg arm, 1.00 210 490 mg arms). simulated absorption-phase half-life (≈16 days ka = 0.0437/day) dominates observed terminal slope single SC dose absorption slower disposition (flip-flop). Steady-state troughs therefore reached several months repeat Q4W dosing.

title: Kotani_2022_astegolimab
text: Covariate distributions. Approximated Table 1 aggregate statistics: weight ~ lognormal centered 79 kg median; CRCL ~ normal centered 87.9 mL/min/1.73 m²; blood eosinophils ~ lognormal centered 180 cells/µL. original subject-level joint distribution public. Dose allocation. 150 subjects per dose arm (70, 210, 490 mg) — balanced. Zenyatta randomized 2:1:1:1 placebo:70:210:490, active per-arm N ≈ 100 ; balanced synthetic cohort chosen VPC readability . Baseline-covariates. Kotani 2022 uses baseline WT / CRCL / blood eosinophils (time-fixed per subject). convention applied event table. ADA status. ADA reported 7.3% patients significant covariate astegolimab PK final model. simulation introduce ADA effect, matching paper. Residual error. Combined additive + proportional natural scale (Cc ~ add(0.603 ug/mL) + prop(0.198)), matching Table 2. IIV Frel. Box-Cox transformed η (shape −2.81) per Eq. 2; η variance square pre-Box-Cox SD reported Table 2 (0.243² = 0.05905).

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Citation: Kovalenko P, Davis JD, Li M, et al. Base Covariate Population Pharmacokinetic Analyses Dupilumab Using Phase 3 Data. Clinical Pharmacology Drug Development. 2020;9(6):756-767. doi:10.1002/cpdd.780 Description: Dupilumab PK model (Kovalenko 2020) Article: Clin Pharmacol Drug Dev. 2020;9(6):756-767 (open access via PMC7496533)

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 pooled 16 clinical studies (N = 2115 participants; 202 healthy volunteers 1913 patients moderate--severe atopic dermatitis (AD)). , 2041 participants active treatment contributed 18,243 20,809 samples population PK analysis. Source studies include Phase 3 AD trials R668-AD-1334 (LIBERTY AD SOLO 1), R668-AD-1416 (LIBERTY AD SOLO 2), R668-AD-1224 (LIBERTY AD CHRONOS). Phase 1 studies R668--0907, TDU12265, PKM14161, R668-AD-1117 used fit “Model 1” - rich-data model file implements. paper’s main text tabulate detailed baseline demographics (age, sex, weight median/range, race breakdown) pooled cohort; appear Supplementary Table S1. approved adult AD maintenance regimen 600 mg SC loading dose day 0 followed 300 mg SC every 2 weeks (Q2W); Kovalenko 2016 precursor publication used 75 kg reference body weight allometric effect central volume, 2020 paper reuses equation without re-stating reference weight. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab")$population.

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Kovalenko 2020 Table 1 (Model 1 column) Supplementary Table S2 (IIV SDs residual errors). paper--implementation cross-walk: paper’s Methods section explicitly defines omega “omega (omega, standard deviation [SD] -subject variability)” sigma “sigma (sigma, SD measurement error)”. nlmixr2’s etalxxx ~ value syntax stores variance (omega^2), Supp. Table S2 SDs squared ini(). Model 1 shrinkage SD reported paper (10.4% / 25.2% / 22.9% / 23.2% / 54.9% Vc / ke / Vm / ka / MTT) consistent IIV--SD accounting.

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 parameterizes linear elimination first-order rate ke * central (CL * Cc) distribution direct rate constants kcp kpc = kcp / Mpc (Q Vp). model file preserves parameterization, derived quantities : Typical clearance: CL = ke * Vc = 0.0534 * 2.48 = 0.132 L/day (matches Table 1 “CL (L/d)”). Typical peripheral rate kpc = 0.213 / 0.686 = 0.310 1/day. etalmtt eta applied MTT = exp(lmtt + etalmtt) (.e. log-normal MTT). Supplementary Table S2 paper reports random effect additively MTT; log-normal implementation chosen avoids negative MTT draws simulation deviation published random-effect structure.

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Detailed observed demographics pooled cohort publicly reproduced paper’s main text. virtual cohort targets labelled adult AD population drawing body weight truncated normal centered Kovalenko 2016 reference weight 75 kg (SD ~18 kg, limits 40-165 kg), matches typical Phase 3 AD trial demographics reported Simpson 2016 SOLO / Blauvelt 2017 CHRONOS publications.
code: set.seed(20260418) n_subj <- 400  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 75, sd = 18), 40), 165) )  # Labelled AD regimen: 600 mg SC loading dose on day 0, 300 mg SC Q2W # for 12 additional doses -> study window 0-182 days. By dose 10+ the # profile is close to steady state (typical half-life ~2-3 weeks). load_dose <- 600 maint_dose <- 300 tau <- 14 n_maint <- 12 dose_days <- c(0, seq(tau, tau * n_maint, by = tau)) amt_vec <- c(load_dose, rep(maint_dose, n_maint))  ev_dose <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time) |>   dplyr::group_by(id) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = amt_vec, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L) |>   dplyr::ungroup()  obs_days <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, tau * (n_maint + 1), by = 1),   dose_days + 0.25,   dose_days + 1,   dose_days + 3 )))  ev_obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT)

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = "WT")

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 Figure 5 illustrates simulated median concentration-time profiles functional dupilumab several SC maintenance regimens (qw, q2w, q4w, q8w). figure reproduces labelled adult AD regimen (600 mg SC loading + 300 mg SC Q2W) 5th/50th/95th percentile bands across virtual cohort, spanning ~13 dosing cycles.
code: vpc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#4682b4") +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_vline(xintercept = dose_days, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey70") +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Dupilumab Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentile profile; 600 mg SC load then 300 mg SC Q2W",     caption = "Virtual AD cohort (N = 400); WT ~N(75, 18) kg, truncated 40-165 kg."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Zoomed-view final Q2W cycle (days 168-182) isolate steady-state peak, trough, AUC_tau used NCA .
code: ss_start <- tau * n_maint # day 168 (time of dose 13) ss_end <- ss_start + tau # day 182  ss_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(ss_summary, aes(time - ss_start, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#b4464b") +   geom_line(colour = "#b4464b", linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time after final dose (days)",     y = "Dupilumab Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Steady-state Q2W cycle (dose 13 of 13)",     caption = "Median and 90% prediction interval over one 14-day dosing interval."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Non-compartmental analysis steady-state Q2W interval (days 168-182). Compute Cmax, Cmin (Ctrough end tau), AUC_tau, average concentration per simulated subject.
code: nca_conc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start,     treatment = "300mg_Q2W_SS") |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = maint_dose, treatment = "300mg_Q2W_SS") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start = 0,   end = tau,   cmax = TRUE,   cmin = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   cav = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■                     40% |  ETA:  2s summary(nca_res) #>  start end    treatment   N     auclast        cmax        cmin         cav #>      0  14 300mg_Q2W_SS 400 1190 [43.9] 95.0 [41.9] 70.4 [49.2] 85.1 [43.9] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; N: number of subjects

what: Comparison against published typical steady-state exposure
title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 tabulate point estimates steady-state Cmax, Ctrough, AUC_tau main text; Figure 5 shows simulated medians qualitatively. Dupilumab FDA labelling follow-PK publications report typical steady-state trough concentrations ~70-80 mg/L 75-kg adult 600-mg-load + 300-mg-Q2W SC regimen. typical-value (“population typical”) prediction IIV zeroed provides direct self-consistency check: Typical-subject steady-state exposure (WT = 75 kg; IIV zeroed).
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  ev_typical <- events |>   dplyr::filter(id == 1L) |>   dplyr::mutate(WT = 75)  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = ev_typical, keep = "WT" ) |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvc', 'etalke', 'etalka', 'etalvm', 'etalmtt'  ss_typical <- sim_typical |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc))  typical_summary <- tibble::tibble(   metric = c("Cmax (mg/L)", "Cmin/Ctrough (mg/L)",     "Cavg (mg/L)", "AUC_tau (day*mg/L)"),   typical_value = c(     max(ss_typical$Cc),     min(ss_typical$Cc),     mean(ss_typical$Cc),     sum(diff(ss_typical$time) *       (ss_typical$Cc[-length(ss_typical$Cc)] +         ss_typical$Cc[-1]) / 2)   ) ) knitr::kable(typical_summary, digits = 2,   caption = "Typical-subject steady-state exposure (WT = 75 kg; IIV zeroed).")

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 Model 1 uses ke/kcp/kpc parameterization rather CL/Q/Vp. model file preserves original parameterization verbatim. Typical values can cross-checked Table 1’s derived CL = ke * Vc = 0.132 L/day. Km F reported fixed Table 1 Model 1, values carried earlier Kovalenko 2016 model; CcaddSd likewise fixed 0.03 mg/L. etalmtt eta applied log-normal MTT (MTT = exp(lmtt + etalmtt)), whereas Supplementary Table S2 implements random effect additively MTT. log-normal form prevents non-physical negative MTT draws simulation. etas match paper’s random-effect structure directly. IIV (omega) reported Kovalenko 2020 standard deviation, per Methods definition. Values ini() squared yield variance nlmixr2 stores ~ operator. earlier version file stored SDs directly RHS (.e. variances), understate IIV magnitude mAb-relevant scale; current file corrected form. reference body weight (75 kg) inherited Kovalenko 2016 (doi:10.1002/psp4.12136); Kovalenko 2020 restate reference weight. See file header comment details. Demographic details (age, sex, race) used Model 1 implementation (WT enters covariate model) re-simulated virtual cohort. Kovalenko 2020 publish numerical Cmax / Cmin / AUC_tau steady state, PKNCA summary self-consistency check implemented model visual appearance Figure 5 rather back--paper numerical comparison. unit conversion required dosing amount (mg) concentration unit (mg/L) mg/L natural unit central/vc .

title: Kyhl_2016_nalmefene
text: Replicate figures 5 publication single 20 mg dose fed state. Assumed mean age, lean body mass, tablet, LC-MS/MS assay used. paper indicates simulations used uniform age distribution 18-80 years LBM ~N(56,72). Since limits provided LBM simulation settings, simulation settings used.
code: dSimDose <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 20, # dose in mg/kg # nolint: commented_code_linter.     TIME = 0,     EVID = 1,     CMT = "depot"   ) dSimObs <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 0,     WT = 5,     TIME = seq(0, 24, by = 0.1),     EVID = 0,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimPrep <-   dplyr::bind_rows(dSimDose, dSimObs) |>   dplyr::mutate(     LBM = 56.28,     AGE = 28,     RIA_ASSAY = 0,     FED = 1,     TABLET = 1   ) Kyhl2016Nalmefene <- readModelDb("Kyhl_2016_nalmefene") # Set BSV to zero for simulation to get a reproducible result dSimNalmefene <- rxode2::rxSolve(Kyhl2016Nalmefene, events = dSimPrep, nStud = 500) dSimNalmefene$Analyte <- "Nalmefene"

title: Kyhl_2016_nalmefene
text: Replicate figure 5 paper. Assuming “confidence bounds” actually 95% prediction intervals.
code: dSimNalmefenePlot <-   dSimNalmefene |>   group_by(time) |>   summarize(     Q025_pk = quantile(sim, probs = 0.025),     Q50_pk = quantile(sim, probs = 0.5),     Q975_pk = quantile(sim, probs = 0.975),     Q025_occ = quantile(e_mu_opioid, probs = 0.025),     Q50_occ = quantile(e_mu_opioid, probs = 0.5),     Q975_occ = quantile(e_mu_opioid, probs = 0.975)   )  ggplot(dSimNalmefenePlot, aes(x = time, y = Q50_pk, ymin = Q025_pk, ymax = Q975_pk)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = "Estimated plasma concentration (ng mL^-1)"   ) +   geom_ribbon(fill = "gray") +   geom_line() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 24, by = 5)) ggplot(dSimNalmefenePlot, aes(x = time, y = Q50_occ, ymin = Q025_occ, ymax = Q975_occ)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = "Occupancy (%)"   ) +   geom_ribbon(fill = "gray") +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = c(60, 90)) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 24, by = 5))

what: Sarilumab PopPK/PD for absolute neutrophil count (ANC)
title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc
text: model semi-mechanistic indirect-response PopPK/PD model sarilumab concentrations stimulate first-order elimination rate absolute neutrophil count (ANC) — mechanistically, margination functional neutrophils circulation vascular wall tissue (Ma 2020). sarilumab PK backbone two-compartment model first-order absorption parallel linear + Michaelis–Menten elimination published Xu et al. 2019 (companion paper sponsor). Ma 2020: Clin Pharmacokinet 59(11):1451–1466 (PMID 32451909). Xu 2019: Clin Pharmacokinet 58(11):1455–1467 (PMID 31055792).
code: mod <- readModelDb("Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc") cat(rxode2::rxode(mod)$reference, sep = "\n") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> Ma L, Xu C, Paccaly A, Kanamaluru V. Population Pharmacokinetic-Pharmacodynamic Relationships of Sarilumab Using Disease Activity Score 28-Joint C-Reactive Protein and Absolute Neutrophil Counts in Patients with Rheumatoid Arthritis. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2020;59(11):1451-1466. doi:10.1007/s40262-020-00899-7 (PMID 32451909). PK backbone: Xu C, Su Y, Paccaly A, Kanamaluru V. Population Pharmacokinetics of Sarilumab in Patients with Rheumatoid Arthritis. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2019;58(11):1455-1467. doi:10.1007/s40262-019-00765-1 (PMID 31055792).

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc
text: ANC model developed 1672 patients rheumatoid arthritis pooled across one phase (NCT01011959), one phase II (NCT01061736 Part ), three phase III studies (NCT01061736 Part B [MOBILITY], NCT01709578 [TARGET], NCT01768572 [ASCERTAIN]); Ma 2020 Table 1. Baseline demographics (Ma 2020 Table 2): mean age 51.7 years (SD 12.1); mean weight 74.1 kg (SD 18.7); 82.2% female; 84.8% Caucasian; 14.2% smokers; 97.7% concomitant methotrexate; 63.8% prior corticosteroid treatment; mean baseline ANC 5.38 × 10⁹/L (Table 4).

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc.R. table collects one place review.

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc
text: original observed data public. cohort draws subjects whose covariate distributions approximate Ma 2020 ANC dataset (Table 2).
code: set.seed(2020) n_subj <- 100 regimens <- c("150 q2w", "200 q2w")  pop <- tibble(   id      = seq_len(n_subj),   WT      = pmax(40, pmin(140, round(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 74.1, sd = 18.7), 1))),   SMOKE   = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.142),   PRICORT = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.638),   regimen = rep(regimens, length.out = n_subj) ) |>   mutate(     dose_mg = ifelse(regimen == "150 q2w", 150, 200)   ) head(pop) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>      id    WT SMOKE PRICORT regimen dose_mg #>   <int> <dbl> <int>   <int> <chr>     <dbl> #> 1     1  81.1     0       1 150 q2w     150 #> 2     2  79.7     0       1 200 q2w     200 #> 3     3  53.6     0       1 150 q2w     150 #> 4     4  53       0       1 200 q2w     200 #> 5     5  40       0       1 150 q2w     150 #> 6     6  87.6     0       0 200 q2w     200

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc
text: Build one event table per subject (dose every 14 days 24 weeks, Cc ANC sampled weekly) solve subject independently, concatenate. per-subject loop sidesteps pre-existing rxode2 issue multi-endpoint models two subjects single rxSolve call abort C solver. call samples fresh set IIV etas model’s omega specification, pooled result equivalent typical “sample N subjects” workflow. deterministic typical-value traces (-subject variability), solve rxode2::zeroRe(). Two typical subjects (one per regimen) fit inside working subset multi-endpoint solver.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc")  sim_one <- function(sub, seed_offset = 0L) {   ev <- rxode2::et(amt = sub$dose_mg, ii = 14, until = 168, cmt = "depot") |>     rxode2::et(seq(0, 168, by = 7), cmt = "Cc") |>     rxode2::et(seq(0, 168, by = 7), cmt = "ANC")   ev_df <- as.data.frame(ev)   ev_df$id      <- sub$id   ev_df$WT      <- sub$WT   ev_df$SMOKE   <- sub$SMOKE   ev_df$PRICORT <- sub$PRICORT   set.seed(2020L + sub$id + seed_offset)   out <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, ev_df, returnType = "data.frame")   out$id <- sub$id   out }  sim <- pop |>   dplyr::group_split(id) |>   lapply(sim_one) |>   dplyr::bind_rows() |>   dplyr::left_join(dplyr::select(pop, id, regimen, dose_mg), by = "id") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> ℹ parameter
