what: Figure 5 — ANC time profiles (200 mg vs 150 mg q2w)
title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc
code: # Replicates Figure 5 of Ma 2020 (ANC time profiles, observed vs predicted, # 200 mg q2w and 150 mg q2w). Black line: typical-value. Ribbon: simulated # 5th/95th percentiles across the virtual cohort. summary_anc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(effect)) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, regimen) |>   dplyr::summarise(     P5  = quantile(effect, 0.05),     P50 = quantile(effect, 0.50),     P95 = quantile(effect, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   )  typ_anc <- sim_typ |> dplyr::filter(!is.na(effect))  ggplot() +   geom_ribbon(data = summary_anc, aes(time, ymin = P5, ymax = P95, fill = regimen), alpha = 0.2) +   geom_line(data = typ_anc, aes(time, effect, color = regimen), linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = expression(ANC~(10^9/L)),     title = "Figure 5 — Simulated ANC vs. time by regimen",     caption = "Replicates the structure of Figure 5 of Ma 2020 (ANC time profiles, 150 vs 200 mg q2w)."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc
code: typ_pk <- sim_typ |> dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc)) ggplot(typ_pk, aes(time, Cc, color = regimen)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Sarilumab Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Typical-value sarilumab PK (Xu 2019 backbone)",     caption = "Companion PK driver for the Ma 2020 ANC PopPK/PD model."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc
text: indirect-response model circulating endogenous species (neutrophils) rather simple drug-PK model, primary validation targets (1) steady-state baseline, (2) dose-response steady state, (3) return baseline drug withdrawal. Classical NCA ANC meaningful. sarilumab PK leg validated PKNCA .

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc
text: sarilumab dose, ANC must sit BASE time. Confirm:
code: ev_placebo <- rxode2::et(seq(0, 56, by = 1), cmt = "ANC") ev_placebo$WT <- 71; ev_placebo$SMOKE <- 0; ev_placebo$PRICORT <- 0 baseline_sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, ev_placebo, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvmax', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka', 'etalbase', 'etalemax', 'etalec50', 'etalkout', 'etalgamma' range(baseline_sim$effect, na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 5.38 5.38

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc
text: Published value: median trough 200 mg q2w predicted ANC reduction baseline 39% (.e. nadir ANC ≈ 0.61 × 5.38 ≈ 3.28 × 10⁹/L). 150 mg q2w: 31% (nadir ≈ 3.71). Simulated ANC nadir percent reduction vs. Ma 2020 reported predictions.
code: ss_ranges <- typ_anc |>   dplyr::filter(time >= 84, time <= 168) |>  # steady state window: weeks 12-24   dplyr::group_by(regimen) |>   dplyr::summarise(     nadir_anc    = min(effect),     trough_anc   = dplyr::last(effect),     mean_anc     = mean(effect),     .groups      = "drop"   ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     pct_reduction_from_base = round(100 * (5.38 - nadir_anc) / 5.38, 1),     paper_reported_pct      = ifelse(regimen == "200 q2w", 39, 31)   ) knitr::kable(ss_ranges, caption = "Simulated ANC nadir and percent reduction vs. Ma 2020 reported predictions.")

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc
text: Ma 2020 reports ANC returns baseline approximately 2 weeks single 200 mg subcutaneous dose. Confirm:
code: ev_single <- rxode2::et(amt = 200, cmt = "depot") |>   rxode2::et(seq(0, 56, by = 1), cmt = "ANC") ev_single$WT <- 71; ev_single$SMOKE <- 0; ev_single$PRICORT <- 0 recovery_sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, ev_single, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvmax', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka', 'etalbase', 'etalemax', 'etalec50', 'etalkout', 'etalgamma'  # Time at which ANC is back within 2% of baseline within_baseline <- recovery_sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(effect), time > 5) |>   dplyr::filter(abs(effect - 5.38) / 5.38 <= 0.02) |>   dplyr::slice(1) within_baseline #>   time vmax    km   vc   cl    ka     q   vp   kel   k12        k21         Cc #> 1   36 8.06 0.939 2.08 0.26 0.136 0.156 5.23 0.125 0.075 0.02982792 0.06494031 #>   base emax ec50 kout gamma     kin        eff      ANC ipredSim      sim #> 1 5.38  1.5 10.3 2.11 0.862 11.3518 0.01879049 5.277737 5.277737 5.277737 #>     depot   central peripheral1   effect CMT WT SMOKE PRICORT #> 1 1.49529 0.1350758    11.22791 5.277737   6 71     0       0

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc
text: Run PKNCA sarilumab concentration leg. paper reports two-fold increase steady-state AUC₀–₁₄ observed escalating 150 200 mg q2w (abstract Xu 2019), consistent saturation non-linear Michaelis–Menten pathway. Steady-state NCA (day 154-168) sarilumab regimen.
code: # rxSolve emits one row per observation record, so with both Cc and ANC # endpoints requested at the same time point we get duplicated (id, time) # rows with identical Cc values. Collapse before handing to PKNCA. sim_pk <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, regimen) |>   dplyr::distinct(id, time, regimen, .keep_all = TRUE)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_pk, Cc ~ time | regimen + id)  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, regimen) |>   dplyr::distinct(id, time, regimen, .keep_all = TRUE) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | regimen + id)  # Steady-state interval: last dose (day 168-14 = 154) to 168 intervals <- data.frame(   start = 154,   end   = 168,   cmax  = TRUE,   cmin  = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   tmax  = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) nca_summary <- summary(nca_res) knitr::kable(nca_summary,              caption = "Steady-state NCA (day 154-168) for sarilumab by regimen.")

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc
text: Kout typo correction (Table 4). Ma 2020 Table 4 prints ANC turnover rate constant > K_out, day⁻¹: Estimate 2.17 (%RSE 35.3, 95% CI 0.638–3.71); > Bootstrap median (95% CI) 211 (1.67–2.88). bootstrap median “211” almost certainly decimal-point error: accompanying 95% confidence interval (1.67–2.88) brackets 2.11 211 (100× discrepancy). neutrophil turnover rate constant ~2/day (half-life ~8 h) physiologically consistent literature neutrophil margination/demargination; 211/day (half-life ~5 min) . package implements Kout = 2.11 (corrected bootstrap median) point estimate, bootstrap CI, physiology agree. reported population-mean estimate (2.17, %RSE 35.3) close 2.11 produces indistinguishable simulated ANC profiles labelled doses. IIV %RSE Kout, also printed Table 4 “227 / bootstrap 232 (198–279)”, retained published. correction documented inline lkout entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc.R. Readers need reproduce (implausible) published bootstrap median exactly can replace log(2.11) log(211) file, though resulting ANC trajectory match Ma 2020 Figure 5. PK backbone Xu 2019, typical-value . sarilumab PK leg uses Xu 2019’s two-compartment + linear + Michaelis–Menten model reference (71 kg female, albumin 38 g/L, CrCl 100 mL/min, CRP 14.2 mg/L, ADA-negative, DP1/DP3). Xu 2019 covariate effects PK parameters (body weight, albumin, CrCl, CRP, ADA, DP2, sex) reproduced file; live companion Xu_2019_sarilumab.R model. sibling DAS28-CRP PD model (Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp.R) makes choice consistency. PK IIV retained (Xu 2019), PD IIV retained (Ma 2020). sets -subject variability carried ini() stochastic VPCs file reasonable. Use rxode2::zeroRe(mod) suppress IIV typical-value plots. Covariate distributions virtual cohort drawn independently marginal distributions Ma 2020 Table 2 (ANC dataset): 14.2% smokers, 63.8% prior corticosteroid, weight ~ N(74.1, 18.7²) truncated [40, 140]. Correlations covariates modelled. Race covariate PD model (84.8% Caucasian Ma 2020 Table 2; RACE_* column required). companion DAS28-CRP model (Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp.R, extracted separately) uses DAS28-CRP column Ma 2020 Table 3. Table 3 known decimal-point errors; typo discussion applies ANC model Table 4.

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Citation: Ma L, Xu C, Paccaly , Kanamaluru V. Population Pharmacokinetic-Pharmacodynamic Relationships Sarilumab Using Disease Activity Score 28-Joint C-Reactive Protein Absolute Neutrophil Counts Patients Rheumatoid Arthritis. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2020;59(11):1451-1466. doi:10.1007/s40262-020-00899-7. PMID: 32451909. PK backbone Xu C, Su Y, Paccaly , Kanamaluru V. Population Pharmacokinetics Sarilumab Patients Rheumatoid Arthritis. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2019;58(11):1455-1467. doi:10.1007/s40262-019-00765-1. Description: Indirect-response PK/PD model sarilumab 28-joint disease activity score C-reactive protein (DAS28-CRP) adults rheumatoid arthritis (Ma 2020). Sarilumab inhibits DAS28-CRP production rate (kin) via sigmoid Emax function includes background DMARD placebo component (PLB). PK driver two-compartment, parallel linear + Michaelis-Menten model Xu 2019 evaluated typical covariate-reference values (adult female, 71 kg, ADA-negative, commercial drug product, ALBR = 0.78, CrCl = 100 mL/min/1.73 m^2, baseline CRP = 14.2 mg/L). Article: Clin Pharmacokinet. 2020;59(11):1451-1466 (PMID 32451909) PK backbone: Xu C et al., Clin Pharmacokinet. 2019;58(11):1455-1467 (open access via PMC6856490); packaged separately Xu_2019_sarilumab library. model DAS28-CRP disease-activity component Ma 2020 joint PK/PD analysis sarilumab rheumatoid arthritis (RA). companion absolute-neutrophil-count (ANC) PD model paper (Ma 2020 Table 4) packaged separately Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc.

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Ma 2020 pooled three pivotal Phase II/III studies DAS28-CRP population PK/PD dataset 2082 RA patients contributing 17,229 DAS28-CRP observations week 24: NCT01061736 Part (MOBILITY Phase II dose-ranging; 12 weeks), NCT01061736 Part B (MOBILITY Phase III; 52 weeks; MTX-inadequate responders), NCT01709578 (TARGET Phase III; 24 weeks; TNF-inhibitor-inadequate responders). Baseline demographics (Ma 2020 Table 2, DAS28-CRP dataset): mean (SD) age 51.6 (12.0) years, 82.1% female, mean (SD) weight 74.6 (18.8) kg (median 72.8 kg per narrative), 83.6% Caucasian. Disease characteristics: mean (SD) baseline CRP 24.1 (25.1) mg/L (median 15.7 mg/L), mean (SD) baseline IL-6 41.8 (67.2) pg/mL, mean (SD) baseline Physician’s VAS 64.6 (16.8) (median 66), mean (SD) baseline HAQ-DI 1.68 (0.640) (median 1.75). Concomitant treatment: methotrexate 98.8%, prior biologic 39.6%, prior corticosteroid 64.6%, baseline-ACCP-positive 16.5%. Dose arms: sarilumab 100/150/200 mg SC Q2W 100/150 mg SC QW, plus placebo. paper tabulate baseline DAS28-CRP mean DAS28-CRP dataset Table 2; companion ANC dataset reported mean 6.03 (consistent modelled typical BASE 6.06 Ma 2020 Table 3). information available programmatically via readModelDb("Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp")$population.

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Every PD parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Ma 2020 Table 3 (final-model column, DAS28-CRP). PK backbone reproduced Xu 2019 Table 3 evaluated typical reference-covariate values (adult female, 71 kg, ADA-negative, commercial drug product, ALBR = 0.78, CRCL = 100 mL/min/1.73 m^2, CRP = 14.2 mg/L); reference covariate values DAS28-CRP model, contrast, set DAS28-CRP-dataset medians per Ma 2020 narrative (CRP 15.7 mg/L, BLPHYVAS 66, BLHAQ 1.75, WT 72.8 kg).

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Logit-transformed Emax. Ma 2020 reports Log(Emax) 0.237 states 55.9% maximum decrease DAS28-CRP. transform consistent logit: Emax = 1 / (1 + exp(-lEmax)) → 1 / (1 + exp(-0.237)) =   0.559 (.e., 55.9%). covariate effect baseline CRP Log(Emax) therefore additive logit scale: lEmax_i = lEmax + etalEmax + theta * log(CRP / 15.7). Indirect response placebo term. inhibitory effect uses sum sarilumab concentration placebo-equivalent term: CeffP = Cc + PLB, PLB concentration units (mg/L). Ma 2020 Figure 1 caption writes Eff(C) = Emax * (C + placebo)^gamma /   (IC50^gamma + (C + placebo)^gamma). baseline (drug) placebo term alone drives Eff non-zero fraction Emax, needed reproduce placebo-arm DAS28-CRP decrease reported Ma 2020 Table 3 narrative (~25% week 24). Baseline carries four continuous covariates. paper’s Table 3 lists CRP, BLPHYVAS, BLHAQ, WT covariates BASE power-form exponents 0.0564, 0.105, 0.0779, 0.0522 respectively. Reference values DAS28-CRP-dataset medians (CRP 15.7 mg/L, BLPHYVAS 66, BLHAQ 1.75, WT 72.8 kg) per narrative. PRICORT Kout multiplier. Table 3’s PRICORT--Kout effect 1.26. paper narrates 0.0333 vs 0.0264 day^-1 PRICORT = 1 vs 0, matches 0.0264 * 1.26 = 0.0333. CV% log-normal variance. IIV Ma 2020 Table 3 Xu 2019 Table 3 reported CV% linear-parameter scale. nlmixr2 convention log-normal IIV log-transformed parameter; conversion omega^2 = log(CV^2 + 1) applied ini(). Residual-error units DAS28. Ma 2020 Table 3 lists additive residual SD 0.647 mg/L, DAS28-CRP score unitless. paper’s CWRES VPC DAS28-CRP units, tabulated value treated additive DAS28-CRP score units ; mg/L Table 3 read copy-paste label carry-adjacent rows. PK backbone without PK covariates. Ma 2020 used sequential individual-PK-Bayes approach: individual PK parameters estimated first Xu 2019, fixed used input PD model. keep model self-contained (approach ()), Xu 2019 PK reproduced typical reference-covariate values PK IIV retained (PK covariate effects). See Assumptions section rationale trade-offs choice.

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: cohort-level simulations use virtual cohort whose covariate distributions approximate Ma 2020 Table 2 demographics DAS28-CRP dataset (n = 2082). Subject-level observed data released paper. Three regimens simulated: placebo (sarilumab zero dose delivering placebo/background-DMARD contribution PLB), 150 mg SC Q2W, 200 mg SC Q2W. horizon extends week 24 — longest follow-reported DAS28-CRP Ma 2020 Table 2 endpoint paper’s week-24 reduction statistics.
code: set.seed(20260419) n_subj <- 60  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id       = seq_len(n_subj),   WT       = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 74.6, sd = 18.8), 40, 170)),   CRP    = pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(15.7) - 0.5 * 1.0^2, 1.0), 0.5),   BLPHYVAS = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 64.6, sd = 16.8), 10, 100)),   BLHAQ    = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 1.68, sd = 0.640), 0, 3)),   PRICORT  = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.646) ) tau      <- 14                           # Q2W dosing interval (days) week24   <- 24 * 7                       # day 168 n_doses  <- ceiling(week24 / tau) dose_days <- seq(0, tau * (n_doses - 1), by = tau) obs_days  <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, week24, by = 1),   dose_days + 0.25,   dose_days + 1,   dose_days + 3,   dose_days + 7 )))

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: model two observation equations (Cc ~ prop(...) sarilumab concentration das28 ~ add(...) DAS28-CRP), combining cohort subjects one rxSolve() call triggers internal rxode2 segfault version used packaging time. vignette therefore simulates subjects one time stitches results together — workaround preserves correctness modest computational cost small (N = 60) validation cohort. deterministic typical-patient comparison paper’s week-24 statistics, zero random effects use reference covariate values (CRP 15.7 mg/L, BLPHYVAS 66, BLHAQ 1.75, WT 72.8 kg, PRICORT = 0):
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  sim_one <- function(subj_row, dose_amt, treatment) {   ev_dose <- subj_row |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_amt, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)   ev_cc <- subj_row |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = "Cc", evid = 0L)   ev_das <- subj_row |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = "das28", evid = 0L)   events <- dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_cc, ev_das) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid,                   WT, CRP, BLPHYVAS, BLHAQ, PRICORT)   out <- as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events))   out$id        <- subj_row$id   out$treatment <- treatment   out }  simulate_cohort <- function(cohort, dose_amt, treatment) {   lapply(seq_len(nrow(cohort)), function(i) {     sim_one(cohort[i, , drop = FALSE], dose_amt, treatment)   }) |> dplyr::bind_rows() }  sim <- dplyr::bind_rows(   simulate_cohort(cohort, 0,   "Placebo"),   simulate_cohort(cohort, 150, "150mg_Q2W"),   simulate_cohort(cohort, 200, "200mg_Q2W") ) mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typical_cov <- tibble::tibble(   id = 1L, WT = 72.8, CRP = 15.7, BLPHYVAS = 66, BLHAQ = 1.75, PRICORT = 0L )  ev_typ <- function(dose) {   ev_dose <- typical_cov |>     tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = dose, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)   ev_cc <- typical_cov |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = "Cc", evid = 0L)   ev_das <- typical_cov |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = "das28", evid = 0L)   dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_cc, ev_das) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid,                   WT, CRP, BLPHYVAS, BLHAQ, PRICORT) }  sim_typ <- dplyr::bind_rows(   as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_typ(0)))   |>     dplyr::mutate(treatment = "Placebo"),   as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_typ(150))) |>     dplyr::mutate(treatment = "150mg_Q2W"),   as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = ev_typ(200))) |>     dplyr::mutate(treatment = "200mg_Q2W") ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvm', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka', 'etalBase', 'etalEmax', 'etalIC50', 'etalKout', 'etalPLB' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvm', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka', 'etalBase', 'etalEmax', 'etalIC50', 'etalKout', 'etalPLB' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvm', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalka', 'etalBase', 'etalEmax', 'etalIC50', 'etalKout', 'etalPLB'

title: Ma_2020_sarilumab_das28crp
text: Approach (): PK backbone embedded typical covariate values. Ma 2020 used sequential individual-PK-Bayes estimates Xu 2019 fixed input PD fit. keep library model self-contained single file, embed Xu 2019 typical PK parameters retain Xu 2019 PK IIV (Vm/CLO/F block, Vc/F, Ka). PK covariate effects (ADA, drug product, sex, ALBR, CRCL, WT--PK, CRP--Vm) omitted. Xu_2019_sarilumab model library full-covariate PK alternative (approach (b)); users need covariate-aware PK PK/PD simulation compose two simulate two steps, using Xu 2019 generate individual sarilumab concentrations feeding input variable simplified indirect-response PD block. Reference-covariate values embedded PK. Xu 2019 typical reference values 71 kg female, ADA-negative, commercial (non-DP2) formulation, ALBR 0.78, CRCL 100 mL/min/1.73 m^2, CRP 14.2 mg/L. internal model exposed user-facing covariates PK side. DAS28-CRP-model reference covariates distinct (CRP 15.7, BLPHYVAS 66, BLHAQ 1.75, WT 72.8, PRICORT = 0) per Ma 2020 narrative. Emax reported Log(Emax) = 0.237 logit transform. paper doesn’t state transform explicitly, Emax = logit^-1(0.237) =   0.559 matches paper’s stated 55.9% maximum decrease DAS28-CRP, whereas exp(0.237) = 1.27 exceed unity biologically implausible fractional inhibition. Covariate--BASE functional form. Ma 2020 Table 3 lists continuous-covariate effects BASE without explicit formula. use standard power form BASE_i = BASE * (cov / ref)^theta, reproduces small % changes described paper narrative (e.g., weight effect ~0.9% 3 kg shift; phyvas effect similar magnitude). reproduce exactly every narrative percentage change single transformation; power form standard choice rheumatology PK/PD modelling matches Ma 2020 anchor values. Residual error unit DAS28. Ma 2020 Table 3 labels additive residual SD 0.647 mg/L, DAS28-CRP endpoint unitless (0-10 scale) paper’s VPC diagnostics plotted DAS28 units. treat 0.647 value additive DAS28 score units interpret mg/L copy-paste carry-adjacent rows Table 3 table cell. Companion ANC model (Ma 2020 Table 4) decimal-point typo. Ma 2020 Table 4 reports Kout bootstrap median "211 (1.67-2.88)" — leading “2” CI lower bound missing decimal. correct bootstrap median Kout 2.11 day^-1 (consistent paper’s narrative half-life bootstrap CI). companion ANC model Ma_2020_sarilumab_anc (task 005) corrects . DAS28-CRP model unaffected. Virtual-cohort covariate distributions. Weight drawn N(74.6, 18.8) kg truncated [40, 170]; CRP log-normal median 15.7 mg/L GSD exp(1.0) ~ 2.7x; BLPHYVAS N(64.6, 16.8) truncated [10, 100]; BLHAQ N(1.68, 0.640) truncated [0, 3]; PRICORT Bernoulli(0.646). ranges approximate Ma 2020 Table 2 drawn observed subject-level data, publicly released. reproduction Ma 2020 Figure 3 typical-patient DAS28-CRP trajectories; (ii) reproduction paper’s week-24 reduction percentages (46.5% / 50.3% 150 / 200 mg Q2W); (iii) baseline placebo-arm sanity checks. Dual observation model. model declares two observation equations: Cc ~ prop(CcpropSd) (sarilumab concentration) das28 ~ add(das28addSd) (DAS28-CRP score). Event tables used rxSolve() therefore need explicit cmt = "Cc" cmt = "das28" observation records disambiguate two outputs; simulation blocks . Per-subject simulation workaround. combining multi-subject event table (two unique IDs) model’s two-output residual-error block, rxode2 version packaging time triggers internal segfault rxSolveSEXP. cohort simulation therefore loops subject--subject binds rows. single-subject simulation IIV zeroed used paper-validation block, unaffected. underlying rxode2 issue fixed upstream, single-call rxSolve(mod, events = cohort_events) reproduce results fraction cost.

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: Citation: Masters JC, Khandelwal , di Pietro , Dai H, Brar S. Model-informed drug development supporting approval avelumab flat-dose regimen patients advanced renal cell carcinoma. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2022;11(4):458-468. doi:[10.1002/psp4.12771](https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12771) Description: Two-compartment population PK model avelumab (anti-PD-L1 IgG1) time-dependent clearance patients advanced solid tumors. Modality: Therapeutic monoclonal antibody (IgG1), IV infusion. Avelumab fully human anti-PD-L1 IgG1 monoclonal antibody. Masters 2022 paper supported approval 800 mg Q2W flat-dose regimen simulating exposure historic 10 mg/kg Q2W weight-based regimen using population PK model inherited Wilkins 2019 (Ref. 20 paper), re-estimated pooled dataset dominated JAVELIN Renal 100 / 101 advanced-renal-cell-carcinoma (aRCC) combination-arm population. Structure: linear two-compartment IV model time-dependent clearance via Hill-type function time since first dose: CL(t)=CLbase⋅(1+Imax⋅tγT50γ+tγ) \mathrm{CL}(t) = \mathrm{CL}_{\text{base}} \cdot   \left( 1 + I_{\max}\cdot \dfrac{t^\gamma}{T_{50}^{\gamma} + t^\gamma} \right) Imax=−0.08533I_{\max} = -0.08533 (fractional decrease t≫T50t \gg T_{50}) allometric weight scaling reference weight 80 kg.

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: final-model population pooled dataset 2,315 subjects across five studies (Masters 2022 Results, Population PK analysis): 488 aRCC patients JAVELIN Renal 100 (Phase Ib, avelumab + axitinib) JAVELIN Renal 101 (Phase III, avelumab + axitinib combination arm) trials. 1,827 solid-tumor monotherapy patients carried prior Wilkins 2019 dataset (metastatic Merkel cell carcinoma, advanced urothelial carcinoma, advanced solid tumors). Demographics aRCC sub-population (Tables S2 / S3 supplementary material): weight range 44.2–143.0 kg, median 81.5 kg; prior monotherapy sub-population: weight range 30.4–204 kg, median 70.6 kg. regimens dosed avelumab 10 mg/kg IV every 2 weeks, 800 mg flat dose simulated alternative. metadata available programmatically via readModelDb("Masters_2022_avelumab")$population.

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Masters_2022_avelumab.R. table collects one place review. Equations: d/dt(central) d/dt(peripheral1): standard two-compartment IV micro-constant form (kel = cl/v1, k12 = q/v1, k21 = q/v2). Hill-type time-dependent CL: Masters 2022 Methods / Results Wilkins 2019 precedent.

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use virtual cohort whose weight distribution approximates aRCC sub-population reported Masters 2022 (range 44.2–143.0 kg, median 81.5 kg). Two dosing regimens compared: weight-based 10 mg/kg Q2W approved flat 800 mg Q2W, given 13 cycles 180 days.
code: set.seed(2022) n_subj <- 200  cohort <- tibble(   ID = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = pmin(pmax(rlnorm(n_subj, log(81.5), 0.22), 44.2), 143.0) ) dose_interval_d <- 14 n_doses         <- 13 dose_times_d    <- seq(0, by = dose_interval_d, length.out = n_doses) obs_times_d     <- sort(unique(c(dose_times_d, seq(0, 180, by = 1))))  build_events <- function(pop, regimen) {   amt_per_subject <- if (regimen == "10 mg/kg Q2W") pop$WT * 10 else rep(800, nrow(pop))   d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(AMT = amt_per_subject) |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times_d) |>     mutate(EVID = 1, CMT = "central", DUR = 1 / 24, DV = NA_real_,            treatment = regimen)   d_obs <- pop |>     tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_times_d) |>     mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DUR = NA_real_,            DV = NA_real_, treatment = regimen)   dplyr::bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(ID, TIME, dplyr::desc(EVID)) |>     as.data.frame() }  events_flat    <- build_events(cohort, "800 mg Q2W") events_wtbased <- build_events(cohort, "10 mg/kg Q2W")

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Masters_2022_avelumab") sim_flat    <- rxSolve(mod, events = events_flat,    returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_wtbased <- rxSolve(mod, events = events_wtbased, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- dplyr::bind_rows(   dplyr::mutate(sim_flat,    treatment = "800 mg Q2W"),   dplyr::mutate(sim_wtbased, treatment = "10 mg/kg Q2W") )

code: sim_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median = stats::median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = stats::quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(time, median, colour = treatment, fill = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Avelumab concentration (ug/mL)",     title = "Simulated avelumab PK: 800 mg flat vs 10 mg/kg Q2W",     subtitle = paste0("Median and 90% prediction interval (N = ",                       n_subj, " virtual aRCC patients per arm)"),     caption = "Model: Masters 2022 CPT:PSP 11(4):458-468"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: Masters 2022 reports fractional decrease CL time (Imax=−0.08533I_{\max} = -0.08533, T50=99.24T_{50} = 99.24 days, γ=2.086\gamma = 2.086). typical-value CL profile reproduces Hill-type time course reference 80 kg subject (deterministic, etas = 0):
code: t_grid <- seq(0, 365, by = 5) events_cl <- data.frame(   ID = 1, WT = 80,   TIME = c(0, t_grid),   AMT  = c(800, rep(NA_real_, length(t_grid))),   EVID = c(1, rep(0, length(t_grid))),   CMT  = "central",   DUR  = c(1 / 24, rep(NA_real_, length(t_grid))),   DV   = NA_real_ ) sim_cl <- rxSolve(   mod, events = events_cl,   params = c(WT = 80, etalcl = 0, etalvc = 0, etalvp = 0, etalImax = 0),   omega  = NA,   returnType = "data.frame" ) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_cl <- sim_cl[sim_cl$time > 0, ]  ggplot(sim_cl, aes(time, cl / cl_base)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "steelblue") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (days)",     y = "CL(t) / CL(0)",     title = "Time-dependent clearance in Masters 2022",     subtitle = "Typical 80 kg subject; asymptotes at 1 + Imax = 0.915"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: Compute NCA parameters third dosing interval (steady-state approximation mAb ~7 day half-life): Simulated NCA parameters (3rd dosing interval, days 28-42)
code: interval_start <- dose_times_d[3] interval_end   <- dose_times_d[4]  sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc),                 time >= interval_start,                 time <= interval_end) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_rel = time - interval_start) |>   dplyr::select(id, treatment, time_rel, Cc)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time_rel | treatment + id)  dose_df <- data.frame(   id        = rep(cohort$ID, 2),   treatment = rep(c("800 mg Q2W", "10 mg/kg Q2W"), each = n_subj),   time_rel  = 0,   amt       = c(rep(800, n_subj), cohort$WT * 10) ) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time_rel | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start     = 0,   end       = dose_interval_d,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   auclast   = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                 52% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■     93% |  ETA:  0s knitr::kable(summary(nca_res),              caption = "Simulated NCA parameters (3rd dosing interval, days 28-42)")

title: Masters_2022_avelumab
text: Table 1 θ_Q typo (0.3352 → 0.03352 L/h): paper’s Table 1 prints point estimate θQ\theta_Q 0.3352 L/h, listed RSE (12.24%) 95% CI (0.02548–0.04157 L/h) internally consistent θ_Q = 0.03352 L/h (missing leading zero printed point estimate). 0.03352 L/h also value inherited Wilkins 2019 base structural model expected range mAb inter-compartmental clearance. corrected value used annotated source-trace comment Masters_2022_avelumab.R. erratum posted publisher site 2026-04-20. Reference weight: Masters 2022 explicitly state reference weight used allometric scaling, 800 mg flat-dose rationale built around approximate 80 kg median body weight, 80 kg used allometric reference CL, V1, V2, Q. Imax parameterization: ImaxI_{\max} always negative source (−0.08533-0.08533). keep every individual Imax,iI_{\max,} strictly negative log-normal IIV, model stores log|Imax|\log|I_{\max}| applies negative sign model() block: Imax_i <- -exp(lImax + etalImax). equivalent log-normal distribution magnitude decrease. ω²(V2) = 1.204 (RSE 109.7%): large log-normal variance (IIV V2 ≈ 135% CV) poor identifiability, internally consistent reported 95% CI (0.9567–1.451) used reported. Downstream NCA estimates driven V2 (e.g., terminal-phase volumes) therefore show wide -subject spread. Residual error convention: sigma column Table 1 standard deviation (variance) proportional (0.1742) additive (2.168 µg/mL) residual components, matching NONMEM’s default reporting. Used directly propSd addSd. Virtual cohort: Demographics simulated log-normal weight distribution anchored aRCC-subpopulation median (81.5 kg) truncated reported range (44.2–143.0 kg). paper tabulate baseline covariates final model body weight enters final structural model. Time units: source reports CL Q L/h T50 days. packaged model keeps time days consistency T50; CL Q converted via ×24.

CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2022;11(9):1244-1255. doi:10.1002/psp4.12846 Description: Two-compartment population PK model etrolizumab first-order SC absorption time-decreasing clearance adults moderately--severely active ulcerative colitis (Moein 2022) Article: https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12846

title: Moein_2022_etrolizumab
text: model developed 1263 subjects (non-missing covariates) pooled five clinical studies etrolizumab adults moderately--severely active ulcerative colitis: ABS4262g (phase ), EUCALYPTUS (phase II), HIBISCUS /II (phase III), HICKORY (phase III), LAUREL (phase III). GARDENIA (phase III) held external validation. Baseline demographics (Moein 2022 Table 3): age range 18-79 years (median 38 years), weight range 38.0-216 kg (median 72.2 kg), 42% female, 45% prior anti-TNF exposure, 23% ADA-positive. Disease extension: 53% left-sided colitis, 42% extensive/pancolitis, 2% , 3% missing. Baseline medians: albumin 41 g/L, CRP 4.31 mg/L, fecal calprotectin 1500 ug/g. Dosing ranged single doses 0.3-10 mg/kg IV (phase ), 0.5-3 mg/kg Q4W SC (phase ), 315-420 mg loading SC (phase II), 4 mg/kg Q4W IV (phase ), phase III regimen 105 mg Q4W SC. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Moein_2022_etrolizumab")$population.

title: Moein_2022_etrolizumab
text: per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Moein_2022_etrolizumab.R. table collects one place. Reference covariate values reference patient (Figure 1 caption): 72 kg WT, 41 g/L albumin, 4.23 mg/L CRP, phase III, prior anti-TNF, left-sided colitis, ADA-negative.

title: Moein_2022_etrolizumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. simulations use virtual populations whose covariate distributions approximate published phase III demographics.
code: make_cohort <- function(n, n_doses = 6, dosing_interval_days = 28,                         obs_days_per_dose = seq(0, 28, by = 2),                         amt_mg = 105,                         seed = 12846) {   set.seed(seed)    # Baseline covariates approximating the phase III population   WT  <- pmax(35, pmin(200, rlnorm(n, log(72), 0.22)))   ALB <- pmax(25, pmin(55, rnorm(n, 41, 4)))   CRP <- pmax(0.1, exp(rnorm(n, log(4.31), 1.2)))   PRIOR_TNF <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.45)   disext <- sample(c("left", "extpan", "other"), n,                    replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.55, 0.43, 0.02))   DISEXT_EP    <- as.integer(disext == "extpan")   DISEXT_OTHER <- as.integer(disext == "other")   # Paper assumes ADA-negative until week 4; use a time-invariant titer of 0   # for the primary cohort and a 97.5th-percentile value for a sensitivity arm   ADA_TITER <- rep(0, n)    dose_times <- seq(0, (n_doses - 1) * dosing_interval_days,                     by = dosing_interval_days)    pop <- data.frame(     ID  = seq_len(n),     WT, ALB, CRP, ADA_TITER, PRIOR_TNF, DISEXT_EP, DISEXT_OTHER   )    # Dose records (depot, SC injections Q4W)   d_dose <- pop[rep(seq_len(n), each = length(dose_times)), ] |>     mutate(       TIME = rep(dose_times, times = n),       AMT  = amt_mg,       EVID = 1,       CMT  = "depot",       DV   = NA_real_     )    # Observation records: per-dose relative sample times, across all doses   obs_grid <- as.vector(outer(obs_days_per_dose, dose_times, "+"))   obs_grid <- sort(unique(obs_grid))    d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n), each = length(obs_grid)), ] |>     mutate(       TIME = rep(obs_grid, times = n),       AMT  = 0,       EVID = 0,       CMT  = "central",       DV   = NA_real_     )    bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>     select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV,            WT, ALB, CRP, ADA_TITER, PRIOR_TNF, DISEXT_EP, DISEXT_OTHER) } mod <- rxode2::rxode(readModelDb("Moein_2022_etrolizumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Moein_2022_etrolizumab
text: Phase III dosing: 105 mg Q4W SC x 6 doses, observations every 2 days 24-week treatment period.
code: events_vpc <- make_cohort(n = 300) sim_vpc <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_vpc) |> as.data.frame()

what: Figure 2 analogue: concentration-time VPC (phase III dose)
title: Moein_2022_etrolizumab
code: d_vpc <- sim_vpc |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q10 = quantile(Cc, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q90 = quantile(Cc, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(d_vpc, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q10, ymax = Q90), fill = "#4682b4", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q10), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q90), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 168, by = 28),                      labels = seq(0, 168, by = 28) / 7) +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (weeks)",     y = expression("Etrolizumab concentration (" * mu * "g/mL)"),     title = "Figure 2 analogue - simulated concentration VPC",     caption = paste0(       "Simulated 10th/50th/90th percentiles, N = 300 virtual subjects, ",       "105 mg Q4W SC x 6 doses.\n",       "Replicates the shape of Figure 2 of Moein 2022 (phase III panels)."     )   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure S3 analogue: typical CL vs. time since first dose
title: Moein_2022_etrolizumab
text: Time-dependent CL per Equation 1 reaches asymptote first 4-8 weeks, stepping dose.
code: # Population-typical CL trajectory for the reference patient (72 kg, 41 g/L # ALB, 4.23 mg/L CRP, no prior TNF, left-sided colitis, ADA-negative). # zeroRe() removes IIV to get typical values. mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() events_typ <- make_cohort(n = 1) |>   mutate(WT = 72, ALB = 41, CRP = 4.23,          ADA_TITER = 0, PRIOR_TNF = 0, DISEXT_EP = 0, DISEXT_OTHER = 0) sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = events_typ,                            keep = c("WT", "ALB", "CRP")) |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalogitmaxred'  ggplot(sim_typ, aes(x = time, y = cl)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, colour = "#4682b4") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 168, by = 28),                      labels = seq(0, 168, by = 28) / 7) +   labs(     x = "Time since first dose (weeks)",     y = "Typical CL (L/day)",     title = "Figure S3 analogue - typical CL vs time since first dose",     caption = paste0(       "Reference patient (72 kg, 41 g/L ALB, 4.23 mg/L CRP, no prior TNF, ",       "left-sided colitis, ADA-).\n",       "Maxred = 26% asymptotic reduction; Onset = 4.8 weeks half-life."     )   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Moein_2022_etrolizumab
text: Compute NCA simulated typical-value profile reference patient: Single-dose phase using first dosing interval (0-28 days) analog phase single-dose pK profile. Steady-state phase using final dosing interval (days 140-168) time-dependent CL reached asymptote. paper reports typical terminal half-lives derived population PK parameters: single dose: 13.0 days (95% CI 12.2-13.9). steady state: 17.1 days (95% CI 16.1-18.3). clean terminal-phase characterization simulate single 105 mg dose dense post-peak sampling, 6-dose steady-state regimen one dense post-final-dose sampling interval.

title: Moein_2022_etrolizumab
text: Single-dose NCA typical-patient profile.
code: # Single 105 mg SC dose, dense sampling out to 84 days (3 half-lives past # the expected 13-day t1/2). events_single <- make_cohort(   n = 1, n_doses = 1,   obs_days_per_dose = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 14, 21,                         28, 35, 42, 49, 56, 63, 70, 77, 84) ) |>   mutate(WT = 72, ALB = 41, CRP = 4.23,          ADA_TITER = 0, PRIOR_TNF = 0, DISEXT_EP = 0, DISEXT_OTHER = 0)  sim_single <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = events_single) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(id = 1L, treatment = "single_105mg") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalogitmaxred'  sim_nca_single <- sim_single |>   filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  dose_single <- events_single |>   filter(EVID == 1) |>   transmute(id = ID, time = TIME, amt = AMT, treatment = "single_105mg")  conc_single <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca_single, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_single_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_single, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals_single <- data.frame(   start     = 0,   end       = Inf,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   aucinf.obs = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  nca_single <- PKNCA::pk.nca(   PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_single, dose_single_obj, intervals = intervals_single) ) knitr::kable(as.data.frame(nca_single$result),              caption = "Single-dose NCA on the typical-patient profile.")

title: Moein_2022_etrolizumab
text: Steady-state NCA final dosing interval.
code: # Six 105 mg SC doses Q4W, with dense post-peak sampling in the final # interval for terminal-phase characterization. events_ss <- make_cohort(   n = 1, n_doses = 6,   obs_days_per_dose = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 14, 21, 28) ) |>   mutate(WT = 72, ALB = 41, CRP = 4.23,          ADA_TITER = 0, PRIOR_TNF = 0, DISEXT_EP = 0, DISEXT_OTHER = 0)  # Add dense post-final-dose terminal-phase samples out to day 252 (84 days # past the final dose, for t1/2 estimation at steady state) final_dose_day <- 5 * 28 extra_obs <- data.frame(   ID = 1L, TIME = final_dose_day + c(35, 42, 49, 56, 63, 70, 77, 84),   AMT = 0, EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DV = NA_real_,   WT = 72, ALB = 41, CRP = 4.23,   ADA_TITER = 0, PRIOR_TNF = 0, DISEXT_EP = 0, DISEXT_OTHER = 0 )  events_ss <- bind_rows(events_ss, extra_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID))  sim_ss <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = events_ss) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(id = 1L, treatment = "ss_105mg_Q4W") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalogitfdepot', 'etalogitmaxred'  sim_nca_ss <- sim_ss |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= final_dose_day) |>   mutate(time = time - final_dose_day) |>   select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  dose_ss <- data.frame(   id = 1L, time = 0, amt = 105, treatment = "ss_105mg_Q4W" )  conc_ss <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca_ss, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_ss_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_ss, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals_ss <- data.frame(   start     = 0,   end       = Inf,   cmax      = TRUE,   tmax      = TRUE,   half.life = TRUE )  nca_ss <- PKNCA::pk.nca(   PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_ss, dose_ss_obj, intervals = intervals_ss) ) knitr::kable(as.data.frame(nca_ss$result),              caption = "Steady-state NCA on the final dosing interval.")

title: Moein_2022_etrolizumab
text: Simulated vs. published terminal half-lives. simulated terminal half-lives fall within 20% published central estimates. single-dose value shorter steady-state value CL maximum (0.260 L/day) first dose drops approximately 0.260 * (1 - 0.263) = 0.192 L/day steady state, lengthening t1/2 proportionally.
code: get_param <- function(res, ppname) {   tbl <- as.data.frame(res$result)   val <- tbl$PPORRES[tbl$PPTESTCD == ppname]   if (length(val) == 0) return(NA_real_)   val[1] }  hl_single_sim <- get_param(nca_single, "half.life") hl_ss_sim     <- get_param(nca_ss,     "half.life")  comparison <- data.frame(   Quantity     = c("Terminal half-life after single dose (days)",                    "Terminal half-life at steady state (days)"),   Published    = c("13.0 (95% CI 12.2-13.9)",                    "17.1 (95% CI 16.1-18.3)"),   Simulated    = c(round(hl_single_sim, 2), round(hl_ss_sim, 2)) ) knitr::kable(comparison,              caption = "Simulated vs. published terminal half-lives.")

title: Moein_2022_etrolizumab
text: PHASE12 covariate residual error implemented. Moein 2022 Table 4 reports -19.2% relative change combined residual error phase /II studies vs. phase III. nlmixr2’s add() / prop() residual-error terms accept per-observation expressions, packaged model uses phase III residual-error magnitude (propSd = 0.196, addSd = 0.427 ug/mL) unconditionally. typical phase III simulations (primary usage pattern) exact; phase /II simulations residual error slightly -stated. future release nlmixr2 supports per-observation error magnitudes, covariate can added. Time-varying ADA titer simplified. Moein 2022 treats ADA titer time-varying covariate. reference patient used source figures (Figure 1) ADA-negative least week 4, ADA_TITER = 0 appropriate reference reproducing published figures. Users simulating ADA-positive patients supply time-series titer values per subject. Reference CRP. model centers CRP 4.23 mg/L (reference- patient value reported Figure 1 caption). population baseline median CRP 4.31 mg/L (Table 3); values produce effectively identical exposures. Virtual covariate distributions. Exact baseline distributions published. demo cohort uses log-normal WT (median 72 kg, CV 22%), normal ALB (41 +/- 4 g/L), log-normal CRP (median 4.31 mg/L, log-SD 1.2), binary prior anti-TNF (45% probability), three- level disease extension (55% left / 43% extpan / 2% ) approximate Table 3 Moein 2022. SC-dosing. packaged model uses SC dosing depot. Moein 2022 also pooled phase IV dose-levels; IV simulations event table dose central directly bioavailability f(depot) = 1 irrelevant. time-dependent CL remains correct time - tad(depot) still resolves time recent dose; dosing IV central, use tad(central) pre-compute TSFD/TAD difference event table.

title: Moein_2022_etrolizumab
text: Moein , Lu T, Jonsson S, et al. Population pharmacokinetic analysis etrolizumab patients moderately--severely active ulcerative colitis. CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 2022;11(9):1244-1255. doi:10.1002/psp4.12846

title: Mould_2007_alemtuzumab
text: Mould et al. (2007) characterised alemtuzumab (Campath®) pharmacokinetics 67 patients B-cell chronic lymphocytic leukaemia (B-CLL) pooled across four studies (CAM002, CAM005, CAM211, CAM213). PK model two-compartment Michaelis–Menten elimination central compartment single time-varying covariate — total white blood cell count (WBC) — entering power effect Vmax. WBC B-CLL patient dominated circulating leukaemic B-cells therefore acts surrogate target-cell burden. Alemtuzumab depletes cells rapidly, WBC (therefore Vmax) falls sharply first weeks treatment. vignette reproduces typical concentration–time profile single 30 mg intravenous infusion 12-week course 30 mg IV three times weekly, documents parameter provenance source-trace table, validates simulated NCA published exposure summaries.

title: Mould_2007_alemtuzumab
text: Mould 2007 Table 1: Race reported published analysis therefore simulated vignette. Weight tested retained covariate final model (Mould 2007, Results). vignette’s virtual population accordingly carry weight covariate PK parameter.

title: Mould_2007_alemtuzumab
text: Every numeric value model file inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Mould_2007_alemtuzumab.R comes following locations Mould DR et al., British Journal Clinical Pharmacology 2007;64(3):278–291 (doi:10.1111/j.1365-2125.2007.02914.x).

title: Mould_2007_alemtuzumab
text: N = 200 virtual CLL patients. Baseline WBC drawn log-normal distribution anchored published median (37.8 × 109/L) -subject SD chosen simulated range covers roughly 1–500 × 109/L, matching Mould 2007 Table 1. Mould 2007 paper pairs PK model indirect-response PD model describes dynamic decline WBC treatment. vignette PD model re-implemented; WBC held baseline value subject throughout simulation. WBC effect Vmax power small exponent (0.194), Vmax mildly sensitive WBC — patient whose WBC falls 40-fold treatment sees Vmax drop factor 400.194 ≈ 2.1 — constant-WBC approximation still captures dominant PK behaviour. deviation documented “Assumptions deviations” section .
code: set.seed(2007) n_subj <- 200  ln_sd <- 1.3 wbc_baseline <- pmax(   0.5,   pmin(     exp(log(37.8) + rnorm(n_subj, 0, ln_sd)),     600   ) )  pop <- tibble(   ID  = seq_len(n_subj),   WBC = wbc_baseline )  summary(pop$WBC) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   1.189  19.606  41.503  82.368  90.826 600.000

title: Mould_2007_alemtuzumab
text: Simulate 12-week course 30 mg IV 3-times-weekly (Monday / Wednesday / Friday) 2-hour infusions. Observations placed pre-dose trough, end--infusion, 6 h 24 h post-dose, dense early grid first 72 h.
code: week_h <- 7 * 24 dose_times <- sort(as.vector(outer(   c(0, 2 * 24, 4 * 24),   (seq_len(12) - 1) * week_h,   `+` ))) tmax_h <- max(dose_times) + 72  obs_only <- sort(unique(setdiff(   c(seq(0, 72, by = 1), dose_times + 2, dose_times + 6,     dose_times + 24, seq(0, tmax_h, by = 24)),   dose_times )))  d_dose <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = dose_times) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 30,     EVID = 1,     CMT  = 2L,     RATE = 30 / 2,     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_obs <- pop |>   tidyr::crossing(TIME = obs_only) |>   mutate(     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = 2L,     RATE = 0,     DV   = NA_real_   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, RATE, DV, WBC)  stopifnot(sum(d_sim$EVID == 1) == n_subj * 36)

title: Mould_2007_alemtuzumab
text: Two passes run: stochastic simulation full IIV residual error (VPC-style plots NCA) typical-value simulation rxode2::zeroRe() reference profile.
code: mod <- readModelDb("Mould_2007_alemtuzumab")  set.seed(20070916) sim_full <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(time_day = time / 24) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'  mod_typ <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim_typ <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typ, events = d_sim) |>   as.data.frame() |>   mutate(time_day = time / 24) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvmax', 'etalkm', 'etalvc', 'etalvp' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

what: Figure-equivalent 1 — Single-dose profile (population and typical patient)
title: Mould_2007_alemtuzumab
text: Mould 2007 Figures 1–2 show individual concentration–time profiles single multiple alemtuzumab doses. panel shows first-dose profile (0–72 h) virtual cohort overlaid typical-value trajectory.
code: first_dose <- sim_full |> filter(time <= 72) first_typ  <- sim_typ  |> filter(time <= 72, id == 1)  ggplot() +   geom_line(     data = first_dose, aes(time, Cc, group = id),     colour = "grey70", alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 0.25   ) +   geom_line(     data = first_typ, aes(time, Cc), colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 1   ) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     x     = "Time after first dose (h)",     y     = expression(Alemtuzumab~concentration~(mu*g/mL)),     title = "Single 30 mg IV 2-h infusion — virtual cohort vs typical patient",     caption = "Mould 2007 Figure 1-equivalent"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Mould_2007_alemtuzumab
text: Mould 2007 (Abstract / Results) reports “maximal trough concentration exceeded 13.2 µg mL−1” patients achieving complete partial response. figure shows simulated trough concentrations 12-week course 13.2 µg mL−1 reference threshold marked.
code: trough <- sim_full |>   filter(time %in% dose_times)  trough_summary <- trough |>   group_by(time_day) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(trough_summary, aes(time_day, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 13.2, linetype = "dashed", colour = "firebrick") +   annotate("text", x = 5, y = 14.5,            label = "Response threshold 13.2 ug/mL",            colour = "firebrick", hjust = 0, size = 3) +   labs(     x     = "Time (days)",     y     = expression(Alemtuzumab~trough~(mu*g/mL)),     title = "Simulated alemtuzumab trough profile over 12-week course",     caption = "Mould 2007 Figure 2-equivalent; 30 mg IV 3x/week"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: PKNCA validation at the final-week steady state
title: Mould_2007_alemtuzumab
text: Run NCA final 48 h dosing interval 12-week course compare typical patient’s simulated AUC0–τ trough 484 µg h mL−1 13.2 µg mL−1 thresholds reported Mould 2007 ≥ 50% probability complete/partial response. PKNCA formula includes grouping variable (treatment) regimen’s NCA results rolled independently, per library’s PKNCA-recipe convention.
code: tau_h <- 48 start_ss <- max(dose_times) end_ss   <- start_ss + tau_h  sim_nca <- sim_full |>   filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= start_ss - 1, time <= end_ss + 1) |>   transmute(     id        = id,     time      = time,     Cc        = Cc,     treatment = "30 mg IV q48h"   )  dose_nca <- d_sim |>   filter(EVID == 1) |>   transmute(     id        = ID,     time      = TIME,     amt       = AMT,     treatment = "30 mg IV q48h"   )  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_nca, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = start_ss,   end     = end_ss,   cmax    = TRUE,   tmax    = TRUE,   cmin    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   cav     = TRUE )  res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals =
